author: martsa

title: Incorrect types for handling Cancel errors @1.4.0
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm not sure what actual problem is, but there are two things that look weird to me:
1. Cancel error triggers .then(), and not .catch()
2. By default axios response type is R = AxiosResponse<T>. But as I said, cancel error is also possible.
So the question is: how should I properly handle cancel errors? I don't have AxiosError type in .then as a result and I can't detect AxiosError without explicitly assigning type of result.

### To Reproduce

You can see types of res in this code in any IDE
```
export function test(abort: AbortController) {
  return axiosInstance("/test", { signal: abort.signal }).then(res => {
    console.log(res); // If request was cancelled, res will be instance of cancel error
    // while res type is AxiosResponse<any, any>
  });
}
```

body:
### Describe the bug

I'm using axios instance

```
const instance = axios.create({
  baseURL: `${BASE_URL}`,
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  withCredentials: true
});
```

When I use instance.post(...) , the parameter in the catch callback is a string : "Request failed with status code 400"

I needed the error object to get the response payload being returned by the server.

For some reason, **if I don't create an instance, and I directly using axios.post('baseURL/api'...) it works fine.**

Trying to use interceptors doesn't solve it.
```
instance.interceptors.response.use(
  response => response,
  error => Promise.reject(error)
);
```

I'm using the latest version available of axios (1.4.0) (it happens also in earlier versions like 0.21.)

just use

```
const instance = axios.create({
  baseURL: `${BASE_URL}`,

  withCredentials: true
});

instance.interceptors.response.use(
  response => response,
```

title: Cookies not sent with withCredentials when data is present
body:
### Describe the bug

When using axios version 1.4.0 or 1.2.4, I've noticed that if there's a server-side authentication logic and data is present in the request body, cookies (specifically token values) are not included in the headers. However, when the data from the request is removed, cookies are sent correctly. Additionally, if the server does not require authentication, the cookies are always sent correctly regardless of the presence of data.

### To Reproduce

1. Set up a server that requires token-based authentication.
2. Send a POST request using Axios with withCredentials set to true and data in the request body.
3. Observe that cookies (tokens) are not sent in the headers.
4. Remove data from the request.
5. Send the request again and observe that cookies are now sent in the headers.


### Code snippet

```js
// Axios code that does not send cookies with data

const apiClient = axios.create({
  baseURL: host,
});

export const postRegister = ({
  type,
  copyrightTitle,
  prompt,
}) => {
  return apiClient({
    method: 'post',
    url: REGISTER_COPYRIGHT_URL,
    withCredentials: true,
    data: {
      type,
      copyrightTitle,
      prompt,
    },
  });
};

```


### Expected behavior

Cookies should be sent in the headers when withCredentials is set to true, regardless of the presence of data in the request.

### Axios Version

1.4.0, 1.2.4

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

Chrome, edge, safari

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

Windows 10, Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7

### Describe the issue

I set `axios.defaults.headers.common['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'`. When firing a `axios.patch` request (without a config), the request uses `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` instead.

### Example Code

```js
// This does not work as expected:
axios.defaults.headers.common['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'

// This does:
axios.defaults.headers.common['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
axios.defaults.headers.patch['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
```


### Expected behavior

I expected POST requests to use `axios.defaults.headers.common`, too.

I think this is where the problem (or intended behaviour) comes from: https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/3f53eb6960f05a1f88409c4b731a40de595cb825/lib/core/dispatchRequest.js#L46

author: twiddler

title: Brotli decompression slow on node, delayed response
body:
### Describe the bug

Fetching data served in brotli compression takes much more time than without brotli.
In the company i currently am working for, it can take 1 second of decompression.
This is due to the axios default accept-encoding header set to 'gzip, compress, deflate, br'

Two ways to solve this case :
If I am on the client side : overriding the accept-encoding header to 'gzip, compress, deflate' solves my problem.
If I am on the server side : disallowing br compression solves my problem.

As the node adapter is used on the server, i wonder if having this 'br' in the header is a good idea since we generally deal with APIs. So the Brotli dictionary should not be very used, leading to the decompression delay.
Or maybe the problem resides in the brotli decompression library.

Well, I put that on this issue in order to let other knows how to deal with slow responses from axios requests.
SEO should do its job by indexing this issue.
Please feel free to close this issue if you want.


### To Reproduce

```
const url = 'https://www.example.com/some/content/from/server/handling/multiple/compressions/including/br';
(async () => {
    const bk1 = Date.now();
    await axios.get(url);
    const bk2 = Date.now();
    await axios.get(url, { headers: { 'accept-encoding': 'gzip, compress, deflat' } });
    const bk3 = Date.now();
    console.log('with br', bk2-bk1);
    console.log('without br', bk3-bk2);
})();
```


### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

br should not be included in the default accept-encoding header of the **node** adapter.

or maybe it's ok like this, but at least, think about it.

### Axios Version

1.2.2

### Adapter Version

HTTP

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

18.16.0

### OS

MacOS 12.3

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

```bash
my measures on a fetched 150Kb content:

- with br: 2144ms
- without br: 151ms
```

title: In version 1.5.0 the Content-type header is application/json instead of multipart/form-data
body:
### Describe the bug

In version 1.4.0 of axios when sending the form data object the content type is properly set.
```
    POST /api/images HTTP/1.1\r\n
    Accept: application/json, text/plain, */*\r\n
    Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=--------------------------908413013073488028571171\r\n
    Authorization: Bearer TEST\r\n\
    User-Agent: axios/1.4.0\r\n
    Content-Length: 117151\r\n
    Accept-Encoding: gzip, compress, deflate, br\r\n
    Host: localhost:8200\r\n
    Connection: keep-alive\r\n
```
When using 1.5.0 though i got this HTTP request
```
    POST /api/images HTTP/1.1\r\n
    Accept: application/json, text/plain, */*\r\n
    Content-Type: application/json\r\n
    Authorization: Bearer TEST\r\n
    User-Agent: axios/1.5.0\r\n
    Content-Length: 4\r\n
    Accept-Encoding: gzip, compress, deflate, br\r\n
    Host: localhost:8200\r\n
    Connection: keep-alive\r\n
```
 
 I call the axios like this
 ```typescript
        const httpAgent = new http.Agent({
          keepAlive: true,
          keepAliveMsecs: 1000,
          maxSockets: 256
        });
       
      const instance = axios.create({
        baseURL: `http://localhost:8200`,
        httpAgent,
      });
    
      instance.defaults.headers.common['Content-Type'] = 'application/json';
      instance.defaults.headers.common['Authorization'] = `Bearer TEST`;
      instance.defaults.maxBodyLength = Infinity;
      instance.defaults.maxContentLength = Infinity;
      
       const payload = new FormData();
       const imageBuffer = fs.readFileSync('path to file')
      payload.append('imageFile', imageBuffer, {filename: 'test.png'});
      const response = await axios.post(`images`, payload);
 ```
 

I think it might be, because of this line:
 ```typescript
       instance.defaults.headers.common['Content-Type'] = 'application/json';
 ```
 if the line above is wrong I think the change should be marked as breaking or if the line is fine, then is should be a bug that should be fixed.

title: Tests No Longer Passing since Upgrading to v1.5.0
body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrading axios from 1.4.0 to 1.5.0, tests that use network calls all fail consistently.

In my tests, I use Vitetest with Pretender intercepting network calls. None of my network tests pass with axios `1.5.0`. I get the following error messages:

```javascript
  code: 'ECONNREFUSED',
  errors: [
    Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:80
        at createConnectionError (node:net:1630:14)
        at afterConnectMultiple (node:net:1660:40) {
      errno: -61,
      code: 'ECONNREFUSED',
      syscall: 'connect',
      address: '127.0.0.1',
      port: 80
    },
    Error: connect ECONNREFUSED ::1:80
        at createConnectionError (node:net:1630:14)
        at afterConnectMultiple (node:net:1660:40) {
      errno: -61,
      code: 'ECONNREFUSED',
      syscall: 'connect',
      address: '::1',
      port: 80
    }
  ],
```

Can confirm this is from axios. Downgrading back to `1.4.0` resolves the problem. 


### To Reproduce

Using the last version of Pretender and an axios instance using v1.5.0, attempt to intercept a network call in a test environment, using Jest or Vitest.

### Code snippet

title: axios timeout s not respected for chunked requests
body: ### Describe the bug Hello dear team. hope you are doing well. I am about to report a bug, although it probably works as expected, then I would ask you to clarify this behavior in the documentation. regarding the axios timeout - it works fine for regular requests. But it is not respected for chunked requests. Imagine the server is streaming the response chunks for 30 sec and I would expect setting 10 sec timeout will abort execution. Instead, it keeps hanging and waiting on the last chunk, so the request can be finished. It is quite non-intuitive and confusing and I wonder if it was designed like that, or you consider it as a bug and we want to fix it. I have attached the github repository with a reproduction example. https://github.com/n0th1ng-else/axios-streaming-timeout could you please take a look? ### To Reproduce https://github.com/n0th1ng-else/axios-streaming-timeout ### Code snippet _No response_ ### Expected behavior The request is aborted after 10 sec due to timeout, even tho it is half way through ### Axios Version 1.5.0 ### Adapter Version _No response_ ### Browser NodeJS express + axios ### Browser Version _No response_ ### Node.js Version 16.16.1 ### OS MacOS Venture 13.4.1 ### Additional Library Versions _No response_ ### Additional context/Screenshots _No response_

body:
### Describe the bug

I'm using axios 1.4.0 in nodejs to post a form and a request interceptor is configured.
To build the form data I use the package "form-data" 4.0.0.
Here's a simplified view of the code:

```
import FormData from "form-data";
import type { AxiosInstance } from "axios";

    const fd = new FormData();
    fd.setBoundary("77f77c04-2c7b-4179-aca3-3e0b66bdb77d");
    const buff = Buffer.from("myTest");
    fd.append("test.json", buff, {
      filename: encodeURIComponent("test.json"),
      contentType: "application/json",
      knownLength: buff.byteLength,
    });
 const response = await this.axios.postForm(`http://localhost:1234`, fd);
```

When the request is sent, the server receive the following header: ``Content-Type, multipart/form-data; boundary=77f77c04-2c7b-4179-aca3-3e0b66bdb77d``

However, when inside my ``requestInterceptor`` the ``Content-Type`` header just ``multipart/form-data`` and it's missing the boundary.

title: BaseURL config vs absolute URL
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I think that less experienced developers are unaware of how Axios behaves in terms of prioritizing absolute URLs over the BaseURL attribute configuration.

 This situation could generate SSRF type vulnerabilities in use cases such as:

```
  config.defaults.BaseURL = 'http://mysite.com';
  path = req.query.path;
  axios.get(path);
```

 A malicious user could enter path values ​​like 'http://evil.com/' (absolute URL) and direct the request to a different site.

### Describe the solution you'd like

 While it is possible to validate that user input does not contain absolute URLs, I find it safer to have a configuration attribute that allows or denies absolute URLs.  Example:

` allowAbsoluteURL = false/true`

 The value _true_ would reflect normal Axios behavior.

 The value _false_ generates an exception in requests when each of the following conditions are met:

- The restclient methods receives an absolute URL as a parameter.
- The value of BaseURL config was explicitly declared in the restclient configuration.
-  The BaseURL value is not a prefix to the absolute URL received as a parameter.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

title: "timeout" config not work on "windows" and "alpine" system
body: ### Describe the bug The above code does not work with the "windows" and "alpine" system node adapters, and the options.timeout value needs to be configured for it to work. It is speculated that it is a platform compatibility issue with the node API. ### To Reproduce ```js const http = require('http') const startTime = Date.now() const req = http.get('http://199.199.199.199', { timeout: 2000 }, () => { console.log('res') }) req.setTimeout(1000, () => { console.log('timeout', Date.now() - startTime) }) ``` The above code sets the timeout to 1000 on "centos" and "mac", and 2000 on "windows" and "alpine". If the timeout configuration in options is removed, the maximum TCP timeout of about 130 seconds will be reached, indicating that req.setTimeout did not take effect. Assuming "199.199.199.199" is a non-existent IP, i.e. an IP that cannot be connected to. ### Expected behavior The http adapter needs to add the following code configuration to take effect on "windows" and "alpine". ### Axios Version 1.4.0 ### Adapter Version http ### Browser _No response_ ### Browser Version _No response_ ### Node.js Version 16 ### OS windows/alpine ### Additional Library Versions _No response_ ### Additional context/Screenshots _No response_

title: Ability to define the response inside a request interceptor
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

My use case is that I want , when I'm in **test** environment, to be able to intercept a request and define "manually" the response. 

For example, my interceptor would be supplied with an `HAR` file, and would be able to generate a response without the client making any actual HTTP request.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would be cool to be able to set the response inside the request interceptor.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I know Jest allow you to mock the full axios module, but a more _integrated_ approach would be nice.

title: Content-Type set to false when sending form data
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm trying to upload a base64 image to my server in my React Native app. The base64 image is first converted to a blob, then is appended to a form data and sent to the server. I'm also explicitly setting the Content-Type as multipart/form-data.

The problem is that the request is not even being sent to the server, with a error: "AxiosError: Network Error".
When I check the AxiosError, I see that the Content-Type is set to false, so I think it is because of it.

![errorNetwork](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/48497295/0c007a67-b2a0-4aef-9581-804325e63315)

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
const form = new FormData();

form.append("62", new Blob([base64String]));

axios.post(url, form, {
  headers: {
    "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data",
    "Accept": "multipart/form-data",
    "Authorization": "..."
  },
}).then(function (response) {
  debugger
 })
 .catch(function (error) {
  debugger
});
```


### Expected behavior

Axios should upload the form data and return a successful response


### Axios Version

1.5.0

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

16.16.0

### OS

Android 11

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
React 18.2.0
React Native 0.71.3
```


### Additional context/Screenshots


I found this issue https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5615 that appears to be the same problem as me.
The issue was closed because the OP managed to make it work using a object instead of a Blob.
When I tried this solution, it still had the same problem of setting Content-Type to false:
```bash
form.append("file", {
  uri: base64String,
  type: 'image/jpeg',
  name: "testepdf"
});
```

When I used a string in the second parameter of append, the Content-Type was set correctly, so it is some problem with object/blob.

### Section/Content To Improve

The path and permalink in `README.md` need to be updated with the latest branch and file path. The permalink should point to the line where the default variables are initialized in `lib/defaults/index.js`.


https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/2ac731d60545ba5c4202c25fd2e732ddd8297d82/README.md?plain=1#L662-L664

### Suggested Improvement

Update the permalink to include the right branch name and point to the proper line of code:

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/main/lib/defaults/index.js#L49

- `master` → `main`
- `L28` → `L49`

Update the file path:

- `lib/defaults.js` → `lib/defaults/index.js`

```bash
README.md
lib/defaults/index.js
```

author: rthangam

title: Support File as body in NodeJS env
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Passing a _File_ (https://nodejs.org/dist/latest-v18.x/docs/api/buffer.html#class-file) as body currently fails with an error saying:
`'Data after transformation must be a string, an ArrayBuffer, a Buffer, or a Stream'. `
_File_ extends _Blob_, but the following check (`if (utils.isBlob(data))`) fails due to the `kindOfTest`:
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/a48a63ad823fc20e5a6a705f05f09842ca49f48c/lib/adapters/http.js#L292C37-L292C37

### Describe the solution you'd like

Passing a _File_ the same way a _Blob_ is passed as a body. Checking `if (utils.isBlob(data) || utils.isFile(data))` should be enough.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

_No response_
author: adzhiljano

title: AxiosHeaders type is difficult to use
body:
### Describe the bug

- `AxiosResponse["headers"]` is typed as `RawAxiosResponseHeaders | AxiosResponseHeaders`.
  - (1) Why wouldn't it always be `AxiosResponseHeaders`?
  - (2) If I wanted to only deal with `AxiosResponseHeaders` (more specifically an instance of `AxiosHeaders`), Typescript won't let me do `new AxiosHeaders(response.headers)`, because `RawAxiosResponseHeaders` is a `Partial`.
- (3) `AxiosHeaders.get` claims to return `string | string[] | number | boolean | AxiosHeaders | null`, forcing callers to deal with any of those types. In reality, isn't `string | string[] | undefined` all you should expect?

### To Reproduce

https://codesandbox.io/s/axios-typescript-headers-issue-64vrrl?file=/src/index.ts

### Code snippet

```js
Copied from codesandbox above:

  const r = await axios.request({
    method: "get",
    url: "https://httpbin.org/response-headers",
  });

  // (1) TS ERROR: `.get` is not callable because `r.headers` may not be a `AxiosHeaders`
  r.headers.get("a");
  // Let's force it...
  // (2) TS ERROR: Nope, that doesn't work either.
  const headers = new AxiosHeaders(r.headers);

  if (!(typeof ha === "string" || Array.isArray(ha))) {
    // (3) TS ANNOYING: Narrows type of `ha` to `number | boolean | AxiosHeaders | null`
    //     In practice, only `undefined` is possible (AFAIK).
    ha;
  }
```


### Expected behavior

- Either `AxiosResponse["headers"]` should always be `AxiosHeaders` (fixes (1)), or `new AxiosHeaders(r.headers)` should be possible (fixes (2))
- The return type of `AxiosHeaders.get` should be stricter than input types, since `AxiosHeaders` normalizes inputs. Specifically, `AxiosHeaders.get` should return `string | string[] | undefined`.

### Axios Version

1.5.1.4

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

N/A

### Browser Version

N/A

### Node.js Version

16.15.1

### OS

N/A

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
N/A
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

```bash
N/A
```

The problem is when we make a request and the response is displayed on the right the form(that is on the left) shrinks and makes the UI look quite bad.  I am attaching screenshots below  do checkout.

**Before making request:**

![Screenshot 2023-10-05 130336](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/72154312/8950f17c-740b-44af-9ac9-d203fb305e6d)

**Also it is not responsive(as evident from the scroll bar at the x axis, it's overflowing content):**

![Screenshot 2023-10-05 131458](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/72154312/02aaaa57-118a-4749-99f5-ddd07f8d48ad)







### Describe the solution you'd like

My solution to this problem is quite simple. 
Instead of aligning them in a grid we can display them in a block that makes it responsive as well as keeping the UI look good.

**My solution solves two problems**:
- Makes the UI look good
- Makes the UI responsive as well

title: FormData entry is emptied when sending multipart POST request (post/postForm)
body:
### Describe the bug

When sending a POST request as `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` (either explicitly or automatically set by Axios), the native `FormData` entry for the file to upload is emptied when serialized to the body right before performing the actual request.

This causes a 415 Unsupported Media Type error when reaching the endpoint.





### To Reproduce

1. Preparing my FormData object, where `file` is a `File` object coming from an `<input type="file">`'s onchange `event.target.files[0]`:
```js
      const formData = new FormData();
      formData.append("file", file);
```
2. Sending it through my post wrapper method:
```js
await service.methods.postEndpointFormDataAsync(
        endpoint,
        formData
      )
```
3. Making the axios POST request (in this case using postForm shorthand function):
```js
postEndpointFormDataAsync: async function (endpoint, formData) {
      return axios
        .postForm(endpoint, formData)
        .then(response => this.handleResponse(response))
        .catch(error => this.handleResponse(error.response));
    }
```

- Or using basic post function with explicit `Content-Type`:
```js
axios
        .post(endpoint, formData, {
          headers: {
            "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data"
          }
        })
        .then(response => {
          return this.handleResponse(response);
        })
        .catch(error => {
          return this.handleResponse(error.response);
        });
```
4. HTTP request sent like:
Header: `Content-Type: application/problem+json`
Body:
```js
{ file: {} }
```

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

- HTTP request sent like:
Header: `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`
Body:
```js
{ file: <binary> }
```

### Axios Version

\>0.27.2

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

Chromium-based

### Browser Version

Latest

### Node.js Version

18.14.0

### OS

Windows 11

author: hamzabouissi

title: CVE-2023-45857 (CWE-359) XSRF-TOKEN value is disclosed to an unauthorised actor
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi team, @jasonsaayman and @DigitalBrainJS,

The library inserts the X-XSRF-TOKEN header using the secret XSRF-TOKEN cookie value in **all requests to any server** when the XSRF-TOKEN cookie is available, and the withCredentials setting is turned on. If a malicious user manages to obtain this value, it can potentially lead to the XSRF defence mechanism bypass.

It's crucial to ensure the protection of CSRF tokens. These tokens should be treated as confidential information and managed securely at all times. 
You may check it here:
[https://portswigger.net/web-security/csrf/preventing](https://portswigger.net/web-security/csrf/preventing)
[https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross-Site_Request_Forgery_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross-Site_Request_Forgery_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html)

Type of vulnerability: [CWE-359: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/359.html)
Severity: High (7.1)  [CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N](https://cvss.js.org/#CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N)

### To Reproduce

1) Start a new project using the latest version of Next.js by running the following command:  `npx create-next-app@latest`. Then, install the latest version of the Axios library with this command: `npm i axios`
2) Create an Axios instance with the following configuration, which enables cross-site request forgery (CSRF) protection by including credentials in requests:
```
  const instance = axios.create({
    withCredentials: true,
  });
```
3) Install the XSRF-TOKEN cookie with specific attributes. Set the cookie value "whatever" and configuring it for the "localhost" domain with strict same-site policy:
```
    const cookies = new Cookies();
    cookies.set("XSRF-TOKEN", "whatever", {
      domain: "localhost",
      sameSite: "strict",
    });
```
4) Initiate a cross-domain request using your Axios instance. In this example, we're making a GET request to "https://www.com/," and we handle the response and potential errors:
```
    instance
      .get("https://www.com")
      .then((res) => console.log(res.data))
      .catch((err) => console.error(err.message));
```
5) Run your project, and open the browser's network tab for debugging and monitoring network activity.
6) Verify that the cross-domain request to "https://www.com/" includes the "X-XSRF-TOKEN" header with the value "whatever." 
7) Confirm that the "XSRF-TOKEN" cookie's value is disclosed to any 3rd-party host when making requests using the Axios instance. **This is essential for security as you don't want to leak CSRF tokens to unauthorized entities.**

### Code snippet

```js
lib/adapters/xhr.js:191
const xsrfValue = (config.withCredentials || isURLSameOrigin(fullPath))
```

### Expected behavior

ER: the XSRF-TOKEN is not disclosed to a 3rd party host 
AR: the XSRF-TOKEN is disclosed in every request made with the Axios instance

### Axios Version

[v0.8.1] - [v1.5.1]

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

### OS

_No response_

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

The current effective solution is to change the default XSRF-TOKEN cookie name in the Axios configuration and manually include the corresponding header only in the specific places where it's necessary.

[https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-45857](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-45857
)
```bash
https://prnt.sc/xDcRmFozxSHJ
```

title: Axios headers.get('set-cookie') yields array of one concatenated cookie string
body:
### Describe the bug

Calling `response.headers.get('set-cookie')` returns an array with one element of form `[ 'cookie1, cookie2, cookie3' ]`.
This is a problem because cookie strings occasionally contain a comma (e.g. in the date of expires: `Expires=Thu, 31 Oct 2024 08:25:24 GMT`)

This has been reported as a bug before in earlier versions but it seems like its back

### To Reproduce

https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/upbeat-hugle-34nf57?file=%2Findex.js%3A9%2C1

### Code snippet

```js
import axios from "axios";

console.log(`Node version: ${process.version}`);
console.log(`Axios version: ${axios.VERSION}`);

const { data, headers } = await axios.get("https://google.com")

console.log(headers.get('set-cookie'));
```


### Expected behavior

I expect that `headers.get('set-cookie')` returns an array where each cookie is an element

1.3.5

title: XMLHttpOverride response.request.responseType value is dropped.
body:
### Describe the issue

**UPDATE**: We're using MSW mocking lib and it seems when it's enabled axios falls back to making calls with `XMLHTTPOverride`. However what I have noticed is the that responseType value is dropped. 

![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/15204499/c0705190-71d8-4727-9ee9-5c686ee134d5)



**Back Story ( Issue is resolve by disabling MSW mocking library )**: 
The issue I'm having isn't something that I've been able to reproduce outside of my application in isolation so I assume the issue is on my end. 

The issue is that when I am making an GET request with a `responseType: "blob"` it's not being honored and the returned filestream `typeof` is `string`.  When I do the same request to the same endpoint in isolation the returned value is an instance of Blob. 

The difference that I see when making the same GET request is that the `response.request` is different when I've tested the call in isolation versus my application. When the `response.request` is `XMLHttpRequest` it honors the passed in option of `responseType: "blob"`. when the `response.request` is `XMLHttpRequestOverride` the `responseType` value is dropped and Axios does not return a Blob. 

My question is what can cause the `XMLHttpRequestOverride`? My test in isolation for the most part mirrors what is in my application but I still can't reproduce it. I appreciate the help here!

![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/15204499/ed69a6cb-dae6-4fb0-8f89-dc5dc8a94acb)


### Example Code

Aside from implementing MSW (MockServiceWork) I'm not sure how to reproduce.

### Expected behavior

I would have expected the responseType value to carry over to the XMLHTTPRequestOverride 

### Axios Version

Tested on 0.27.2 and 1.6.0

### Adapter Version

Chrome

118.0.5993.117

18-20

title: FormData upload broken after update to msw  v2
body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrading msw from 1.x to 2.x `FormData` upload stopped working in tests. Looking at the debugging output, Axios doesn't append the `boundary` to the `Content-Type` header anymore.

The MSW team checked [1] this already on their side.

I tried to append the boundary manually to the header for debugging purposes, but that revealed another issue:
```
{ "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data; boundary=abc" }
```
ends in
```
Content-Type: multipart/form-databoundary=abc
```


[1] https://github.com/mswjs/msw/issues/1835


### To Reproduce

Checkout the msw reproducer at [1] and
```
cd examples/with-vitest
npm install
npm test
```


[1] https://github.com/pschyma/msw-examples/tree/axios-formdata-reproducer

title: Application fails to require the axios package
body:
### Describe the bug

```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'charAt')
    at resolveURL (/Users/dennymueller/IdeaProjects/forest-school-saxony/node_modules/axios/dist/node/axios.cjs:3241:44)
    at standardBrowserEnv (/Users/dennymueller/IdeaProjects/forest-school-saxony/node_modules/axios/dist/node/axios.cjs:3247:17)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/dennymueller/IdeaProjects/forest-school-saxony/node_modules/axios/dist/node/axios.cjs:3260:5)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1254:14)
    at Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1308:10)
    at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1117:32)
    at Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:958:12)
    at Module.require (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1141:19)
    at require (node:internal/modules/cjs/helpers:110:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/dennymueller/IdeaProjects/forest-school-saxony/node_modules/@nestjs/axios/dist/http.module.js:16:33)
``` 

The error above causes my application to fail upon boot.
This error explicitly happens when using the latest version 1.6.1 NOT with 1.6.0.
And it only happens when i use the production build of my application not in dev mode.

I am using a combination of webpack, nestjs, angular with angular-universal and domino as mock for ssr.

### To Reproduce

Checkout the project:
https://github.com/denny99/axios-ticket.git

Run:
```
npm ci
npm run build
npm start
```

Crashes with the error message that the axios package is not installed.
This is statement is not quite correct. Its caused as the require of axios fails.
For easier debugging, i've added a `require('axios')` in backend/main.ts line 12.

author: ghost

title: The code change that was made in 1.6.0 was this (from #6028):
body:
- SPA on app.mydomain.com
- API on api.mydomain.com

...i.e. where the API does the backend auth and issues CSRF tokens which are then used by the SPA. This is a common software architecture.

### Proposal

Add a `trustedDomains` setting or something similar to Axios config which the CSRF can be sent to (i.e. when request target domain <> request origin domain).

...unless there is a better fix of course

body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Barring a migration guide for Axios `1.x`, the latest `0.x` version of Axios should continue to receive updates for non-breaking security vulnerability resolutions.

### Describe the solution you'd like

[CVE-2023-45857](https://www.mend.io/vulnerability-database/CVE-2023-45857) affects versions `0.8.1` thru `1.5.1`. The fix applied to the `1.x` versions in #6028 should be applied to the `0.x` branch as well, and released as a patch.

title: Breaking change in 1.6.1 release does not allow to create axios instance in tests
body:
### Describe the bug

When testing the application and trying to mock the axios (e.g. axios-mock-adapter, but generally any `axios.create()` call) I get error "TypeError: Invalid URL", which is caused by the fact that the change of the 1.6.1 of the function `hasStandardBrowserEnv` is missing the original conditions `typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof document !== 'undefined';` in  the function `isStandardBrowserEnv`. That leads to the attempt to create and read url path from the document location, which is eventually not initialized yet. 

### To Reproduce

In my case I find it with stencil: 

```
npm init stencil
npm i axios-mock-adapter
npm i
```

in file `.../src/components/my-component/my-component.spec.ts` add 

```js
import axios from 'axios';
import MockAdapter from 'axios-mock-adapter';

const mockAdapter = new MockAdapter(axios);
```


### Code snippet

```js
import axios from 'axios';
import MockAdapter from 'axios-mock-adapter';

const mockAdapter = new MockAdapter(axios);
```


### Expected behavior

Either to have possibility to enforce "nonstandardBrowser" or to preserve behaviour of version 1.6.0

title: Hashtag not escaping - iOS 17
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios ".get(" request does not escape hashtag. It happened on iOS 17 - iOS 16 works good.

### To Reproduce

We define Instance
export const AxiosInstance = axios.create({
  baseURL: APP.baseURL,
  headers: {
    Authorization,
    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
  },

Next we run
return AxiosInstance.get(query);

query has "q=${search}" as param. All characters are escaping well, but not hashtag.

If we escape hashtag manually to %23. Axios escapes %23 to %2523.

### Code snippet

1. we have Axios instance
export const AxiosInstance = axios.create({
  baseURL: APP.baseURL,
  headers: {
    Authorization,
    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
  },
});

2. we send ".get(URL)" with it

3. URL has special characters for one param.

If we insert "#football test" - it escapes " " space but not "#".

If we manually escape "#" to "%23" - Axios will escape that "%" from %23 to "%25" so it will look like "%2523"

There is no way to make encoding before Axios because Axios will escape URL again after we send it.

So Axios must escape "#" as well and it works in iOS 16 - I checked on my phone with iOS 16, but it does not work in iOS 17 - checked with simulator and my friend checked it with his phone

### Expected behavior

_No response_

1.6.2

_No response_

title: Axios request with baseUrl + url redirects to 127.0.0.1:80
body:
### Describe the bug

When using the default axios instance to create a request, when providing a valid `baseUrl` and `url`, it sends off a request to 127.0.0.1:80 instead of the intended location.

Works fine if only providing a fully qualified `url` parameter.

### To Reproduce

```js
const axios = require("axios");
const baseUrl = "http://localhost:18181";
const url = "/dummy/api/route";

const response = await axios({
      method: "get",
      baseUrl,
      url,
      // ^^^ uncomment to view bug ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:80
      // url: `${baseUrl}${url}`,
      // ^^^ uncomment to view fix ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18181
      proxy: false,
    });
```

```js
try {
                        const data = fs.readFileSync(path);
```

author: yimuyangshu

title: "ReferenceError: navigator is not defined" in require('axios')
body:
### Describe the bug

There is an unprotected use of `navigator` global object recently introduced (1.6.2) that can cause crash on NodeJS

To be more precise: when `navigator` is undefined but `window` and `document` are not.

1 - considering the following code in \dist\esm\axios.js

`      const hasBrowserEnv = typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof document !== 'undefined';
`

`hasBrowserEnv` evaluates to `true`

2 - considering the following code in \lib\platform\common\utils.js

```
    const hasStandardBrowserEnv = (
    (product) => {
      return hasBrowserEnv && ['ReactNative', 'NativeScript', 'NS'].indexOf(product) < 0
    })(typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.product);

```

`(typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.product)` evaluates to `false`

which make `hasStandardBrowserEnv` evaluate to `(true && (-1) < 0)` that is `true` 

The consequence is that with `navigator` undefined but `window` and `document` both defined
**THE MERE LOADING OF** lib\helpers\isURLSameOrigin.js will evaluate following code :

```
      export default platform.hasStandardBrowserEnv ?
      // Standard browser envs have full support of the APIs needed to test
      // whether the request URL is of the same origin as current location.
        (function standardBrowserEnv() {
          const msie = /(msie|trident)/i.test(navigator.userAgent);
          ...

```

that causes a **"ReferenceError: navigator is not defined"** error.

@DigitalBrainJS what do you think?

Suggested fix: in lib\helpers\isURLSameOrigin.js replace code with
`        const msie = /(msie|trident)/i.test(navigator && navigator.userAgent);
`

### To Reproduce

node -e "window = document = {}; require('axios')"

### Code snippet

Real code that was failing:
```js
    const { JSDOM } = require('jsdom');
    const { window } = new JSDOM('<html><body></body></html>');

    global.$ = global.jQuery = require('jquery')(window);

    global.window = window;
    if (global.document === undefined) {
        global.document = window.document;
    }

    ... losts of other things ...

    require('axios'); // Crashes...
```

### Expected behavior

Do not crash... especially during load

### Axios Version

at least 1.6.2

### Adapter Version

-

### Browser

none, or JSDom fake browser

### Browser Version

N.A.

### Node.js Version

any

### OS

any

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
-
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

```bash
-
```

title: Credentials on redirection are not being sent.
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm running this on my frontend to make a request in my application

```
logout: async function() {
  	const response = await axios.get('/logout', {withCredentials: true});
  	return response.data;
  }
```

The issue is, `/logout` redirects to an external site and axios is not setting the credentials (cookies in this case) stored in the browser for the external site. CORS is configured correctly and the redirect works with `fetch()`, url shorteners and navigating to /logout directly. Why doesn't axis support proper authentication on redirections? title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in axios/axios
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS). When a manipulated string is provided as input to the format method, the regular expression exhibits a time complexity of O(n^2). Server becomes unable to provide normal service due to the excessive cost and time wasted in processing vulnerable regular expressions.

Please visit https://huntr.com/bounties/69a1aa07-c36e-4d9e-9325-b634e0aa4bb0/

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
const axios = require('axios');

console.time('t1');
axios.defaults.baseURL = '/'.repeat(10000) + 'a/';
axios.get('/a').then(()=>{}).catch(()=>{});
console.timeEnd('t1');

console.time('t2');
axios.defaults.baseURL = '/'.repeat(100000) + 'a/';
axios.get('/a').then(()=>{}).catch(()=>{});
console.timeEnd('t2');


/* stdout
t1: 60.826ms
t2: 5.826s
*/
```


### Expected behavior

A regex optimization

### Axios Version

1.6.2

### Adapter Version

XHR/HTTP

### Browser

Chrome

### Browser Version

_No response_

20.10.0

Linux

[axios](https://www.npmjs.com/package/axios) is a promise-based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js.

An attacker can deplete system resources by providing a manipulated string as input to the format method, causing the regular expression to exhibit a time complexity of O(n^2). author: KaKi87

title: URL Encoded Value is Wrong
body:
### Describe the bug

When passing a value in the params of an Axios call, Axios automatically URL encodes special characters in the provided value before sending it to the respective server. However, I encountered an issue while implementing a feature for our project. For example, when attempting to pass the value "**test (20%**" in the Axios params, Axios encodes it as "**test%20(20%**", which is incorrect. Notably, even web browsers are unable to decode this value, resulting in an "Unable to decode value" error. The accurate URL-encoded representation should be "**test%20%2820%25**", as decoding this value on the server side yields the intended result, "**test (20%**"

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

It should give encoded value for **test (20%** is this **test%20%2820%25** not  this**test%20(20%**

My use case involves processing a PDF file, so the upload, processing, and download take a while. Sometimes the server stops sending the response for 'n' reasons (e.g., restart due to being OOMKilled), leaving the client hanging.
The Axios timeout configuration is mapped to `request.setTimeout`, which measures the inactivity since a socket is assigned. Therefore, it should be set no less than the time between the connection and the first byte response.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I'd like to set an activity timeout for the response data/packet flow.
Additionally, I'd greatly appreciate a connect timeout, addressed by #4325.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

author: gnesher

title: Current version of follow-redirects is vulnerable
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The current version of the `follow-redirects` package has knows vulnerabilities and gets flagged by our security hub. Current version containing this vulnerability is 1.15.0. 

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation due to the improper handling of URLs by the `url.parse()` function. When `new URL()` throws an error, it can be manipulated to misinterpret the hostname. An attacker could exploit this weakness to redirect traffic to a malicious site, potentially leading to information disclosure, phishing attacks, or other security breaches.

### Describe the solution you'd like

This vulnerability is fixed as of 1.15.4, please bump up the version in the axios package in order to solve this. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

author: SubrotoWipro

title: dns lookup upgraded in 1.6 incorrectly attempts to resolve address in case of an error from the lookup callback
body:
### Describe the bug

TL;DR: if you supply custom DNS `lookup` in 1.6+ and it fails with `ENOTFOUND`, `axios` throws `TypeError: address must be a string` instead

I supply custom DNS `lookup` to precisely control the timeout. I am using https://github.com/szmarczak/cacheable-lookup. It works correctly in axios before 1.6 but fails after because of this change:

![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/498673/50a0e6ba-7fe0-4b03-b5c9-6b05d7b4cfa1)

When the callback returns the `err` as the first argument, the other two can be (and are) `undefined`. Axios 1.6+ attempts to resolve `family` and `address` regardless of `err` and trips over `undefined`.

Take a look:

![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/498673/5ee84a2e-361b-4bd0-a589-85d81fe9208e)


### To Reproduce

Perform axios request against a domain that does not exists with a custom `lookup`. DNS `lookup` fails with `ENOTFOUND` but `axios` then fails with `TypeError: address must be a string`

### Code snippet

```js
// simplified test:


import CacheableLookup from 'cacheable-lookup';
import axios from 'axios';

const dns = new CacheableLookup({ resolver: new Resolver({ timeout: 200 }), maxTtl: 60 * 60, lookup: false });
const controller = new AbortController();

await axios.post('https://no-such-domain-987654.com', {}, {
  signal: controller.signal,
  lookup: dns.lookup.bind(dns),
});
```

### Expected behavior

`axios` should fail with the DNS `lookup` error, not the `TypeError`

### Axios Version

1.6+

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

### OS

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

title: version 1.6.4 and later causes authentication to fail
body:
### Describe the bug

Version 1.6.4 and later will sometimes cause the basic authentication to fail, seemingly randomly. When passing the credentials of an http request, sometimes the server will return 401 error. So far it seems to happen to only certain endpoints of the same server (Flask), even if the requests are otherwise identical. Reversing to version 1.6.3 fixes the issue. After some digging, it seems to be caused by the follow-redirects module version 1.15.4.  Downgrading the module to version 1.15.3 will also fix the problem. 
Current workaround without downgrading is to pass the Authorization header instead of the auth property in configuration when making a request.

title: follow-redirects should not be used in browser
body:
### Describe the issue

Hiya,

I'm the maintainer of your dependency `follow-redirects`, getting inbound since your switch to our `1.15.4` that this version does not work in browsers:
- https://github.com/follow-redirects/follow-redirects/issues/237
- https://github.com/follow-redirects/follow-redirects/pull/238

The `follow-redirects` library must not be used in browsers; it is bringing browser functionality to Node. It has no effect in the browser and must not be used.

Some users of Axios might be doing the wrong thing somehow. I just wanted to let you know.

title: Axios aborting API request triggers unhandled error in Sentry
body:
### Describe the issue

While using Axios for making API requests, encountering a situation where the request gets aborted if the user navigates to another tab or refreshes the page during the API call. The problem arises when this aborted request results in an unhandled error, which Sentry consumes, causing an increase in error counts. Can someone help me by sharing the way to handle the request aborted error, which sentry should not consume as an unhandled error?

**Actual Behavior:**
The Axios request is being aborted, leading to an unhandled error that Sentry captures and logs.

### Axios Source Code :

```js
// Handle browser request cancellation (as opposed to a manual cancellation)
request.onabort = function handleAbort() {
  if (!request) {
    return;
  }

  reject(createError('Request aborted', config, 'ECONNABORTED', request));

  // Clean up request
  request = null;
};
```


### Expected behavior

The Axios request should handle scenarios where the user navigates away or refreshes the page, preventing the error from being flagged as unhandled in Sentry.

### Axios Version

1,6,7

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

Firefox

122.0 

### Node.js Version

20

title: Header Content-type : 'multipart/form-data' deleted when sending form data
body:
### Describe the bug

in my project recently install react-native-canvas and react-native-webview , but suddenly axios not working because content-type in header got deleted when sending formData, i found similiar issue with this problem but not on react-native, so currently i fix this with patch-package to xhr.js file to workaround this issue. maybe because axios detecting browser (react-native-webview) that why this causing issue.

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
diff --git a/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js b/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js
index 26126b2..73673f5 100644
--- a/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js
+++ b/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js
@@ -64,9 +64,10 @@ export default isXHRAdapterSupported && function (config) {
     let contentType;
 
     if (utils.isFormData(requestData)) {
-      if (platform.hasStandardBrowserEnv || platform.hasStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv) {
-        requestHeaders.setContentType(false); // Let the browser set it
-      } else if ((contentType = requestHeaders.getContentType()) !== false) {
+      // if (platform.hasStandardBrowserEnv || platform.hasStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv) {
+      //   requestHeaders.setContentType(false); // Let the browser set it
+      // } else 
+      if ((contentType = requestHeaders.getContentType()) !== false) {
         // fix semicolon duplication issue for ReactNative FormData implementation
         const [type, ...tokens] = contentType ? contentType.split(';').map(token => token.trim()).filter(Boolean) : [];
         requestHeaders.setContentType([type || 'multipart/form-data', ...tokens].join('; '));
```


### Expected behavior

no need to deleted content-type on react-native

1.6.2

author: mrentsch65

title: TypeError: EventEmitter is not a constructor
body:
Thank you to all those who contribute to this project!

In some of our projects, our team is finally attempting to migrate from axios@0.27.2 to a current revision.

We have had some issues during runtime - that generated an error:
`EventEmitter is not a constructor`

This traced to the file:
`axios/lib/adapters/http.js`

Based on our testing, it looks like it may be some sort of integration incompatibility of axios, esm-wallaby and readable-stream

Looking the imports, and contrasting it to:
https://nodejs.org/docs/latest-v18.x/api/events.html#class-eventemitter

We were able to resolve the issue by adjusting the syntax specified there to import to match what was in the API for ESM syntax

i.e. switching from:
`import EventEmitter from 'events';`
to:
`import { EventEmitter } from 'events';`

(I'll note this also worked:  `import { EventEmitter } from 'node:events';`)

Here is the diff that solved my problem:

```diff
diff --git a/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js b/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js
index 21d6458..b9fe277 100755
--- a/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js
+++ b/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js
@@ -19,7 +19,7 @@ import fromDataURI from '../helpers/fromDataURI.js';
 import stream from 'stream';
 import AxiosHeaders from '../core/AxiosHeaders.js';
 import AxiosTransformStream from '../helpers/AxiosTransformStream.js';
-import EventEmitter from 'events';
+import { EventEmitter } from 'events';
 import formDataToStream from "../helpers/formDataToStream.js";
 import readBlob from "../helpers/readBlob.js";
 import ZlibHeaderTransformStream from '../helpers/ZlibHeaderTransformStream.js';
```

title: Upgrade to axios version > 1.6.0 is resulting in error "You may need an appropriate loader to handle this file type"
body:

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.

- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest axios version.
- If you need a new feature there's a chance it's already implemented in a [library](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/ECOSYSTEM.md) or you can implement it using [interceptors](https://github.com/axios/axios#interceptors).
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

**⚠️👆 Delete the instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️**

-->

#### Summary

Upgrade to axios version > 1.6.0 is resulting in error "You may need an appropriate loader to handle this file type".
I have installed all the necessary dependencies. Tried modifying the babel and webpack configs, still no luck.

following is the webpack config -

const defaultWebpackConfig = (env = {}, argv = {}) => {
  const production = argv.p;
  const processPath = process.cwd();
  const rootPath = processPath.includes("packages")
    ? processPath.split("packages")[0]
    : processPath;

  const rules = [
    {
      test: /\.(tsx|ts)?$/,
      exclude: /node_modules/,
      use: {
        loader: "ts-loader",
        options: {
          transpileOnly: true,
        },
      },
    },
    {
      enforce: "pre",
      test: /\.[tj]sx?$/,
      use: "source-map-loader",
    },
  ];
  const devConfig = {
    context: path.join(__dirname, "src"),
    entry: {
      "babel-polyfill": "@babel/polyfill",
      "terra-mixins": "terra-mixins",
      "dynamic-forms": path.resolve(path.join(rootPath, "src/index")),
    },
    plugins: [
      new HtmlWebpackPlugin({
        template: path.join(rootPath, "src", "site", "index.html"),
      }),
      new NamedChunksPlugin(),
      new ForkTsCheckerWebpackPlugin(),
    ],
    resolve: {
      extensions: [".ts", ".tsx", ".json", ".js"],
    },
  };
  if (production) {
    devConfig.plugins.push(
      new DefinePlugin({
        "process.env.NODE_ENV": JSON.stringify("development"),
      })
    );
  }
  const config = merge(defaultWebpackFunction(env, argv), devConfig);
  config.module.rules.splice(1, 0, ...rules);
  return config;
};

downgrading axios version is out of the options since the older versions have vulnerabilities

#### Environment

 - Browser [e.g. Chrome, Safari]

author: ghost

title: Security vulnerability in `follow-redirects`
body:
### Describe the bug

Versions of the package follow-redirects before 1.15.4 are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation due to the improper handling of URLs by the url.parse() function. When new URL() throws an error, it can be manipulated to misinterpret the hostname. An attacker could exploit this weakness to redirect traffic to a malicious site, potentially leading to information disclosure, phishing attacks, or other security breaches.

More info:
https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-26159
https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-FOLLOWREDIRECTS-6141137

title: Wrong default value on responseType parameter in Request Config document
body:
### Section/Content To Improve

https://github.com/axios/axios?tab=readme-ov-file#request-config
The default value of responseType is `json`. But I found the default object defined in https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/defaults/index.js has no responseType, in other words, the responseType value is undefined.


### Suggested Improvement

In node adapter, the default response format is data string when request with `data:` schema  or string when request with normal schema; and in browser adapter, it will been returned in string format default. 
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/d844227411263fab39d447442879112f8b0c8de5/lib/adapters/http.js#L221-L263
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/d844227411263fab39d447442879112f8b0c8de5/lib/adapters/http.js#L535-L591
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/d844227411263fab39d447442879112f8b0c8de5/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L100-L101

### Relevant File(s)

```bash
README.md
lib/defaults/index.js
lib/adapters/http.js
lib/adapters/xhr.js
```

title: Axios Modifies ID Field in JSON Response When Nested Inside Projects Array
body:
### Describe the bug

I've encountered an issue with Axios where it appears to be modifying the id field in a JSON response when the id field is nested inside a projects array. This behavior is unexpected and causes inconsistency in handling JSON responses.

### To Reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Make a GET request to an API endpoint using Axios.
2. Ensure that the response contains a projects array with an id field nested inside.
3. Log the response data to the console.
Expected Behavior:
The id field within the projects array should remain unchanged and match the original value in the JSON response.

Actual Behavior:
Axios appears to modify the id field within the projects array. For example, if the original id value is 123, Axios may convert it to 124 or another modified value.

### Code snippet

```js
const axios = require('axios');

axios.get('https://example.com/api/projects')
  .then(response => {
    console.log(response.data);
  })
  .catch(error => {
    console.error('Error:', error);
  });
```

title: [TypeScript] `response.headers.hasContentType()` raises 2 illegitimate TS errors
body:
### Describe the bug

`response.headers.hasContentType()` (where `response: axios.AxiosResponse<any, any>`) raises 2 illegitimate TS errors (_although this expression works as expected_):

```
Cannot invoke an object which is possibly 'null' or 'undefined'. ts(2723)
```
```
This expression is not callable. ts(2349)
  Not all constituents of type 'string | number | boolean | AxiosHeaders | string[] | ((matcher?: AxiosHeaderMatcher | undefined) => boolean)' are callable.
    Type 'string' has no call signatures.
```

### To Reproduce

e.g. https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/lucid-dawn-82cn7d

- Run `tsc` in a Terminal to raise the TS errors.
- Run `node src/index.js` to confirm that the code works.

### Code snippet

```js
import axios from "axios";
const response = await axios.post("http://httpbin.org/post", { x: 1 });

console.log(response.headers.hasContentType("application/json"));
console.log(response.headers.hasContentType("application/xml"));
```


### Expected behavior

`response.headers.hasContentType()` (where `response: axios.AxiosResponse<any, any>`) shouldn't raise any TS error and be recognized as a boolean by TypeScript.

1.6.7

Request cancellation via AbortSignals is failing due to a runtime error caused by an incorrect variable name. This bug was introduced in this PR https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4659 where the variable name `req` is being used in the context where it should `request`.

title: paramsSerializer is not working in v0.28.0
body:
### Describe the bug

Although there's a backport in #6227, in v0.28.0, the serializer isn't working as expected. This is because the v0.28.0 version of `buildUrl` ([code](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/3b7635aefc842c05da0ec8c90e8bd09cb54616b8/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L36-L40)) is simply only taking `options.encode`, not taking `options.serializer` into account:

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/ce46346c0f5306b65023bba324c76fe5fe83eee8/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L36-L44

This seems to be a refactor to match the v1.x version, because in v0.27.2 at least ([code](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v0.27.2/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L28-L30)), it takes the `paramSerializer` function into account:

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/bc733fec78326609e751187c9d453cee9bf1993a/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L28-L30

In order for #6227 to actually work, there needs to be a backport similar to #5113 towards v0.x

### To Reproduce

- Set the version to v0.28.0
- Set the `paramsSerializer` function
- It won't work

### Code snippet

```js
// Simply following https://axios-http.com/docs/req_config

axios.create({
  paramsSerializer: function (params) {
    return Qs.stringify(params, {arrayFormat: 'brackets'})
  },
})
```


### Expected behavior

The parameters should be serialized, but it didn't

### Axios Version

v0.28.0

author: startjava

title: Typing issue in AxiosResponseHeaders
body:
### Describe the bug

`AxiosResponseHeaders` suggest that common headers are written in upper-case:

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/d844227411263fab39d447442879112f8b0c8de5/index.d.ts#L92

while at runtime, they are always lower-cased, as seen in this issue:

- https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/413#issuecomment-239245178

trying to access `response.headers?.['Cache-Control']` yields `undefined` at runtime.

### To Reproduce

https://www.typescriptlang.org/play?#code/JYWwDg9gTgLgBAbzgQQB7AgZwEoFNOQB2mucAvnAGZQQhwDkAhulvQFBsDGEx8U+REgC4ULHAJ4k4AXjiEArgBtFcRplWEAnh2684AC1yMAJrijrZ-ApNwA6QybOY2AehdwPcAHoB+IA

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

I think the types should reflect how things work at runtime, and while we can access all headers in any casing because `RawAxiosHeaders` are just `[key: string]: AxiosHeaderValue`, I think the `CommonResponseHeadersList` should be changed to show lower-cased header names:

```diff
- type CommonResponseHeadersList = 'Server' | 'Content-Type' | 'Content-Length' | 'Cache-Control'| 'Content-Encoding';
+ type CommonResponseHeadersList = 'server' | 'content-type' | 'content-length' | 'cache-control'| 'content-encoding';
```


### Axios Version

1.3.3

### Adapter Version

_No response_

20.5.1

### Describe the bug

In 0.28.0 there is a breaking change to params serialization that wasn't mentioned as such in the changelog.

Probably related to c05ad4895293ac8e0bbb90b28e0772571a97cdd5 and 807918bda2768df0f3a3422abd4e30d16694092d

@DigitalBrainJS

Send a get request with one of the `params` values being an object with key/value properties.

```js
axios.get('foo', {
```

title: Progress event does not have lengthComputable property
body:
### Describe the bug

I use axios to send requests in the browser. I recently updated from axios v0.22.0, and I'm noticing that `onUploadProgress` works a little differently from how it did before — enough that I had to change my code. Previously, the `onUploadProgress` callback was passed a native `ProgressEvent`. It's now passed an `axiosProgressEvent`. I like `axiosProgressEvent` and all the new properties available on it. However, there's one property that the `ProgressEvent` had that the `axiosProgressEvent` doesn't: `lengthComputable`. Previously, my code was using `lengthComputable`, so I had to change it. That wasn't hard to do, but I didn't realize I had to for a while, as I didn't see this change mentioned in the release notes. Going forward, I'll probably just use the `progress` property on the `axiosProgressEvent`, but it would have been nice not to change existing code.

`lengthComputable` is still accessible via the `event` property on the `axiosProgressEvent`, but I think it should also be a top-level property on the `axiosProgressEvent`. That would make `axiosProgressEvent` more like a superset of a native `ProgressEvent`.

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
onUploadProgress: (event) => {
  // event no longer has a lengthComputable property.
  console.log(event);
}
```


### Expected behavior

I was expecting the `lengthComputable` property to continue to exist on the event passed to `onUploadProgress`. If it's possible, I think it'd be helpful to add `lengthComputable` to the `axiosProgressEvent`. Alternatively, this change could be highlighted in the release notes. I think this change was introduced in bdf493cf8b84eb3e3440e72d5725ba0f138e0451 as part of v1.0.0.

### Axios Version

1.6.2

XHR

title: Form data key of "x-amz-security-token" changes from string key to object {x: { amz: {security: { token: <value> } } } }
body:
### Describe the bug

When using axios post and formData for an aws call, payload changes form froma string key to a nested object.

I have these 5 keys in the formData, `key`, `AWSAccessKeyId`, `x-amz-secuity-token`, `policy` and `signature` with respective values. These are all added to a formData object.

When i make a post call with axios via: `await axios.post(url, formData);` the key `x-amz-secuity-token` is transformed to the following on the network request:
```
x: {
   amz: {
      secutiy: {
         token: <value>
      }
}
```

This causes the network request to fail. When i run the same request with fetch via:
```
await fetch(url, {
                method: "POST",
                body: formData,
              });
``` 
The request succeeds as expected and the formData is unchanged in the payload.

### To Reproduce

Run `await axios.post(url, formData);` with formData that has a key similar to `x-amz-security-token` (the dashes) and inspect the payload, the formData object should transform for the key specified into an object.

A similar call with fetch succeeds without transforming the formData:

```
await fetch(url, {
                method: "POST",
                body: formData,
              });
``` 


### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

I expect the formData to remain intact when performing the call instead of transforming into an object.

title: Why is the headers attribute of InternalAxiosRequestConfig mandatory?
body:
### Describe the issue

The headers attribute in AxiosRequestConfig is optional, and InternalAxiosRequestConfig inherits from AxiosRequestConfig, but its headers attribute is required.

The InternalAxiosRequestConfig interface is inherited when encapsulating axios, but its headers attribute is required, resulting in new AxiosHeaders() being assigned to it every time it is used.

Why is it designed?

title: Information Exposure due to the handling of the Proxy-Authorization header across hosts
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

-->

#### Describe the issue

We encountered the following security issues: 
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure due to the handling of the Proxy-Authorization header across hosts. When using a dependent library, it only clears the authorization header during cross-domain redirects but allows the proxy-authentication header, which contains credentials, to persist. This behavior may lead to the unintended leakage of credentials if an attacker can trigger a cross-domain redirect and capture the persistent proxy-authentication header.
When will a new version be released to address this security issue?
#### Axios Version
 @1.6.5

title: Security: Axios sends HTTPS data in cleartext to a proxy (regression)
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios use the HTTP proxy incorrectly, so that the data and URL is sent in plaintext to the proxy. The proxy then wrongly tries a TLS connection with the target server and fails.

Older axios versions work correctly. Other libs also work.

### To Reproduce

1. Set proxy environment variables, using `export http_proxy="http://foo:3100"` and export https_proxy="http://foo:3100" (the http:// is correct, because the HTTP client connects to the proxy via HTTP and does HTTPS on top)
2. `let r = await axios.get("https://www.beonex.com"); console.log("page", r.text());`



### Code snippet

```js
// with proxy, see above
let r = await axios.get("https://www.beonex.com");
console.log("page", r.text());
```


### Expected behavior

* The client connects to the remove server directly via TLS. The squid proxy only forwards the TCP stream, and is *not* involved in the TLS connection
* The proxy should not even see the URL (!), only the hostname.
* You see the HTML source of the webpage


### Actual behavior

* Error HTTP 503
* You see the HTML source of the squid proxy error page
* TLS connection settings mismatch between squid proxy (!) and remote server
* Page, with HTML and some text removed for readability:
"
The following error was encountered while trying to retrieve the URL: https://www.beonex.com/

Failed to establish a secure connection to [unknown]

TLS code: SQUID_TLS_ERR_CONNECT+GNUTLS_E_KEY_USAGE_VIOLATION
SSL handshake error (SQUID_TLS_ERR_CONNECT)

This proxy and the remote host failed to negotiate a mutually acceptable security settings for handling your request. It is possible that the remote host does not support secure connections, or the proxy is not satisfied with the host security credentials.
"
* You can see that the target URL is in the proxy error page. That's a serious security bug. (The proxy should never see the full URL in the first place. Ditto headers, cookies etc.)


### Axios Version

1.5.1

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

18.15.0 (but not relevant, see below)

### OS

Ubuntu

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

### Cause

The squid proxy should not even be involved in the TLS connection. By the very definition of it, HTTPS needs to connect straight from the browser to the target server, and no intermediate proxies should be involved in the TLS connection. Otherwise, that would defeat the entire purpose of the TLS connection, to prevent man in the middle to see the content. That's what the `CONNECT` is for in HTTP proxies: It simply tunnels the TCP connection, and the browser does TLS.

It appears that axios is driving the HTTPS proxy wrong and doesn't do a `CONNECT`, but lets the proxy connect to the target server via TLS. That would be wrong.

### What works

* Axios 0.27 works, axios 1.5.1 fails, so it's clearly a regression sometime in this version range. This also proves that it's a bug in axios. I haven't been able to narrow down the first faulty version yet that broke it.
* [ky](https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky) (version 1.2.3) works fine, again showing that it's likely a bug in axios.

title: Axios Unable to Handle "204 No Content" Responses
body:
### Describe the bug

As of today, Axios seems to be unable to handle requests that result in a "204 No Content" response. Any API request that responds with a 204 status code is treated as a timeout.

Preliminary testing and investigation suggest that the issue might be related to Axios instances.

It might be challenging to provide a publicly accessible test case since finding APIs that reliably respond with a 204 status code could be difficult.

### To Reproduce

As of the latest observation, Axios encounters an issue where responses with an empty body (e.g., "204 No Content") are incorrectly treated as timeouts when using an Axios instance.

Notably, when requests are made without creating an Axios instance directly, the behavior is as expected.

Steps to Reproduce:
- Create an Axios instance.
- Make a request using the Axios instance to an API endpoint that returns a response with an empty body (e.g., 204 status code).
- Observe that the Axios instance incorrectly handles the response as a timeout.

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

Axios should accurately handle responses with an empty body, such as 204 status codes, without treating them as timeouts.

### Axios Version

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

title: [0.28.1] options.paramsSerializer as function don't work
body:
### Describe the bug

We have a custom serialize method that is passed to **axios** by `options.paramsSerializer`.
It's work fine with axios@0.28.0, but from axios@0.28.1, it throw the error :
> options must be an object


### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
const conf = {
  paramsSerializer: function (params: any) {
    return qs.stringify(params, { arrayFormat: 'repeat' })
  }
}

const instance = axios.create(conf)
instance.request()
```


### Expected behavior

The HTTP request is executed (like in 0.28.0).

### Axios Version

0.28.1

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

### OS

_No response_

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

The problem come from the commit [111811eadc849c08a3a2074930c50f68afa6eba5](https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/111811eadc849c08a3a2074930c50f68afa6eba5).

In **axios.js**, this check was added :
```js
if (paramsSerializer !== undefined) {
  validator.assertOptions(paramsSerializer, {
    encode: validators.function,
    serialize: validators.function
  }, true);
}
```
And `validator.assertOptions` throw the error when the first parameter isn't a object.

Moreover, the next line manage the case where `paramsSerializer` is a function :
```js
utils.isFunction(paramsSerializer) && (config.paramsSerializer = {serialize: paramsSerializer});
```

A solution will be to down the check after the below line transform `paramsSerializer` from function to object, so the check will be ok.

title: [0.28.1] options.paramsSerializer as function don't work
body:
### Describe the bug

We have a custom serialize method that is passed to **axios** by `options.paramsSerializer`.
It's work fine with axios@0.28.0, but from axios@0.28.1, it throw the error :
> options must be an object


### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
const conf = {
  paramsSerializer: function (params: any) {
    return qs.stringify(params, { arrayFormat: 'repeat' })
  }
}

const instance = axios.create(conf)
instance.request()
```


### Expected behavior

The HTTP request is executed (like in 0.28.0).

### Axios Version

0.28.1

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

### OS

_No response_

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

The problem come from the commit [111811eadc849c08a3a2074930c50f68afa6eba5](https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/111811eadc849c08a3a2074930c50f68afa6eba5).

In **axios.js**, this check was added :
```js
if (paramsSerializer !== undefined) {
  validator.assertOptions(paramsSerializer, {
    encode: validators.function,
    serialize: validators.function
  }, true);
}
```
And `validator.assertOptions` throw the error when the first parameter isn't a object.

Moreover, the next line manage the case where `paramsSerializer` is a function :
```js
utils.isFunction(paramsSerializer) && (config.paramsSerializer = {serialize: paramsSerializer});
```

A solution will be to down the check after the below line transform `paramsSerializer` from function to object, so the check will be ok.
