author: swetha8612

title: postForm's form-data serialization breaks for Blob in Node.js
body:
### Describe the bug

When I try to have `postForm` automatically serialize a `Blob` in Node.js, it fails with the error message `"Blob is not supported. Use a Buffer instead."` I dug into it a bit, and it seems the culprit is a combination of things.

### To Reproduce

1. Install the latest version of Node.js (at the time of writing: v21.7.3) and 
2. Run a server that accepts file uploads at `POST /api/upload` (or whatever endpoint you'd like)
3. Have an image file in the path `/tmp/image.jpg` (or wherever you'd like)
4. Run the code below in Node.js

### Code snippet

```js
const filename = "/tmp/image.jpg"

axios.postForm(
	"/api/upload",
	{
		"files": {file: await fs.openAsBlob(filename), filename}
	}
)
```


### Expected behavior

`postForm` serializes the object containing the `Blob` as `FormData` and uploads it successfully

### Axios Version

1.6.8

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

Node.js

### Browser Version

v21.7.3

### Node.js Version

v21.7.3

### OS

macOS 14.4.1

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
N/A
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

On [lib/helpers/toFormData.js:92](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/helpers/toFormData.js#L92), `formData` gets instantiated as `PlatformFormData`, which comes from the [`form-data`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/form-data) package. This probably shouldn't happen, since Node.js has its own built-in `FormData`. I'd suggest swapping the order of that `||` statement to have any environment's built-in `FormData` take precedence.

There's a long-standing bug in `form-data`, https://github.com/form-data/form-data/issues/396, wherein `FormData[Symbol.toStringTag]` is `undefined`. `FormData[Symbol.iterator]` is also `undefined`, but that's not covered in the issue. However, it is relevant here. The function `isSpecCompliantForm` ([lib/utils.js:632](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/utils.js#L632)) uses these to check whether whatever's getting passed in (`thing`) is indeed an instance of `FormData`. I'd suggest doing that a different way, such as using `instanceof`.

What does all this have to do with `Blob`? Well, for some reason that's beyond me, in `toFormData`, `isSpecCompliantForm` is tied to `useBlob` ([lib/helpers/toFormData.js:110](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/helpers/toFormData.js#L110)), the boolean that determines whether or not `Blob` is supported. In fact `Blob` is supported, by virtue of the fact that `_Blob` is defined in the previous line. I'd suggest decoupling these expressions, since bundling together `FormData` validation and `Blob` validation is too complex for one boolean, especially when the name of that boolean only references one of them. It unnecessarily breaks `Blob` support based on a bug in a third-party implementation of `FormData`—which, as you'll recall from earlier, we probably shouldn't even prefer to use in the first place.

Another question that comes to mind is: why do we even need to validate `FormData` at all here? Since we're already preferring a specific version of a third-party implementation of `FormData`, I think we can safely assume that what we have on our hands is actually a spec-compliant `FormData` (except, of course, for the finer details of `Symbol.toStringTag` where `form-data` differs from `FormData`). The only reason I can think of to validate `FormData` is if (as we should) we preferred to use the environment's built-in `FormData`, which might differ between environments. And doing that would actually fix this bug altogether.
author: exchgr

title: Axios stream aborts early during 204 response from prism cli.
body:
### Describe the bug

Using Axios as part of my API tests to make calls to an API Gateway. To validate these calls match the API spec they are being routed through the prism proxy (https://docs.stoplight.io/docs/prism/72d69fb629de0-validation-proxy)

When testing some DELETE endpoints that return http 204 Axios just abruptly fails and is unable to capture the response.
It throws this exception, mentioning that the maxContentLength has been exceeded even though no maximum has been specified.
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/46888066/16ef905a-79af-4a43-827f-5370f3745ba0)
It seems like this error message is just a generic one that is shown when the stream is aborted. As the condition for it to be shown shouldn't occur when no max is set.
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/46888066/d6056d62-9d26-4297-99e5-64f4fd442c9f)
This is also confirmed because if I do provide a limit, the error messages still shows even though I'm not possibly exceeding the limit.
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/46888066/4779e976-41a1-4c4d-8a08-94daa6bcda17)

I think there issue is somewhere between prism proxy and axios, as based on the prism logs the request is being forwarded to the API, processed and returned, its just Axios throws this exception when processing the return.

I captured the request coming back from the prism proxy using wireshark and it looks like this:
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/46888066/3887b84d-8b86-4e23-9a61-b3490ebaca7e)

and i can see in the prism logs that request succeeded
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/46888066/29d7ed88-08a3-49e5-a948-5c916059524d)



### To Reproduce

title: 100% Upload progress on dev mode only.
body:
### Describe the bug

A similar issue ([#3469](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/3469)) published on Dec, 2020 but now marked closed without a clear fix.
Problem: When using `onUploadProgress` in dev mode you will always have a 100 progress, I've seen this issue in multiple projects.
- Note that in production build the progress will be calculated correctly. However, In dev we sometimes need to see the progress to adjust some UI, etc ..

### To Reproduce

Calculate the upload percentage using onUploadProgress and display it in dev mode. You will notice always that the `progressEvent`'s `progress `attribute is a constant 1, and `loaded` attribute have the final size of the file always, that's why the percentage will always be 100%.

author: ahmed-khlifi

title: XSRF-TOKEN logic should utilize cookie domain instead of current page domain
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When using `axios` to make HTTP calls to a backend server from a ui hosted at a different subdomain, the XSRF-TOKEN logic is not available and requires manually parsing out the cookie and passing the X-XSRF-TOKEN header yourself. This is because the logic that currently exists checks to see if the full domain of the page you're currently on matches the full domain of the api you're making a call to. 

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/ab3f0f9a94853c821cb00f1112788ecdd3ae7ed1/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L191
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/ab3f0f9a94853c821cb00f1112788ecdd3ae7ed1/lib/helpers/isURLSameOrigin.js#L55-L59

### Describe the solution you'd like

Instead of matching against the current page and the target api url, this logic could match the target api url against the XSRF-TOKEN cookie domain. Since the XSRF-TOKEN cookie will be sent along with the request to this domain regardless, there should no problem in also pulling out the cookie value and passing it as the X-XSRF-TOKEN header. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

The alternative having the developer utilizing axios ensure that each axios call to the backend domain passes the withXSRFToken config as true, or building this logic into a withXSRFToken function config that does the cookie/target domain match itself.

### Additional context/Screenshots

Having static files for a Single-page Application (React) hosted via a CDN at a different domain than the backend is a relatively common practice which would benefit from this change.

[!] RollupError: Node tried to load your configuration as an ES module even though it is likely CommonJS. To resolve this, change the extension of your configuration to ".cjs" or pass the "--bundleConfigAsCjs" flag.

The problem is occurring on this line in `rollup.config.js`:

const lib = require("./package.json");

title: fetch adapter: Streaming request bodies is not supported [react-native]
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello,

First of all, many thanks to @DigitalBrainJS for adding the fetch adapter!

There's one thing that I noticed while trying the latest release [v1.7.0-beta.0](https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/6373), It seems to be breaking my react native app. I've added the following packages and followed the instructions to add the required polyfills

https://github.com/react-native-community/fetch?tab=readme-ov-file
https://github.com/acostalima/react-native-polyfill-globals

Unfortunately, that didn't seem to help. Not sure if there's a workaround or a way to avoid using `ReadableStreamer` at least for RN since it doesn't seem to be supported natively from my understanding. As pointed out under the [Motivation section](https://github.com/acostalima/react-native-polyfill-globals?tab=readme-ov-file#motivation):

> [ReadableStream](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ReadableStream) is not supported and, by extension, [Response.body](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Body/body) is not implemented. Related discussions:
https://github.com/facebook/react-native/issues/27741
https://github.com/facebook/react-native/issues/12912
https://github.com/JakeChampion/fetch/issues/198
https://github.com/JakeChampion/fetch/issues/746

For reference, here's a screenshot of the error running on an iOS simulator on iOS 17.0

![Simulator Screenshot - iPhone 15 Pro - 2024-04-30 at 14 33 30](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/25246005/03a6c095-1e4c-4fb6-9fe2-3b42886d3432)

Spin up a new RN project

`yarn add axios@next`

Install the required polyfills from:

title: Add support for configuring the frequency of progress update events
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

It would be useful to be able to control the frequency of download progress update events. According to MDN, the browser standard for XHR is at most once every 50ms, while axios is using a mixture of every 300ms or every 500ms depending on whether we use the XHR, Fetch, or http adapter.

### Describe the solution you'd like

A new config option in the `AxiosRequestConfig` - something like `progressUpdateIntervalMs` which will determine the minimum amount of time between progress updates (progress updates can take longer if no progress is made, this is expected behaviour)

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

This is because per spec (https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#methods) methods are case-sensitive, and only some methods are normalized, but patch is not one of them.

The solution is to call `method.toUpparcase()` on 2 places:
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/adapters/fetch.js#L125
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/adapters/fetch.js#L149


Http and xhr adapters do this, probably because of the same reason mentioned above:
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/adapters/http.js#L164
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L33

title: `null` parameters are getting passed in urls breaking requests in v0.x
body:
This PR to backport custom params serializer from v1 into v0: https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/6263

does not include the v1 fix outlined below (spotted by @FlorisVeldhuizen) around `null` params being pushed into the URL.

It looks like the changes to `toFormData.js` were not included in the backport: https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/b0ebf9fcacbab3e5a020a1a4d5d3837342d7dcd7#diff-8423b44acd3fb9f35b00404bfeed15aaf4659cdf06b879849c5026b4a05d2efa 

axios v0.28.1 will send blank params:

```
{
  foo: null,
  bar: 1
}

?foo&bar=1
```

axios v0.27 does not send blank params

```
{
  foo: null,
  bar: 1
}

?bar=1
```

>> It seems that `null` and `undefined` parameters are now getting passed as well in urls, breaking many requests. You can see this check being removed in #4734, I wonder how this breaking change that is not mentioned anywhere went under the radar

> Good catch; I wonder if we can update the main meat of their toString function for serializing the params to omit undefined and null. Feel free to make suggestions on #5108! I just wanted some of the old functionality back 😅

_Originally posted by @ChronosMasterOfAllTime in https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5107#issuecomment-1276295059_

title: adapter fetch should pass fetchOptions to fetch function
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When I use `fetch` as `adapter` in Nodejs, there is (maybe) no way to use `ProxyAgent` as dispatcher provided by [undici](https://github.com/nodejs/undici?tab=readme-ov-file#undicifetchinput-init-promise) (which is the default implication of fetch API since [node v18 released](https://nodejs.org/en/blog/announcements/v18-release-announce#fetch-experimental)).

I expect the code below should work and send request through proxy `http://127.0.0.1:8888`, however it doesn't.

```
const axios = require("axios");
const { ProxyAgent } = require("undici");

axios
    .get(
      "http://127.0.0.1:4000",
      {
        adapter: "fetch",
        fetchOptions: {
          dispatcher: new ProxyAgent("http://127.0.0.1:8888"),
        }
      }
    )
```

### Describe the solution you'd like


After checking the source code in `lib/adapters/fetch.js` and found `fetchOptions` may need pass to `fetch()` as the second paramater.
```
export default isFetchSupported && (async (config) => {
  let {
    url,
    method,
    data,
    signal,
    cancelToken,
    timeout,
    onDownloadProgress,
    onUploadProgress,
    responseType,
    headers,
    withCredentials = 'same-origin',
    fetchOptions
  } = resolveConfig(config);

  // ...
  request = new Request(url, {
    ...fetchOptions, // <- here the fetchOptions pass to Request
    signal: composedSignal,
    method: method.toUpperCase(),
    headers: headers.normalize().toJSON(),
    body: data,
    duplex: "half",
    withCredentials
  });
  
  let response = await fetch(request); // <- not pass to fetch
```

So I pass `fetchOptions` to `fetch()`, it works. And run axios test it all passed. Also I've read the whatwg's fetch spec, I think the fetchOptions pass to `fetch()` as second param should work exactly the same as pass to `Request`.
```
  request = new Request(url, {
    signal: composedSignal,
    method: method.toUpperCase(),
    headers: headers.normalize().toJSON(),
    body: data,
    duplex: "half",
    withCredentials
  });

  let response = await fetch(request, fetchOptions);
```

However, After read the whatwg's [fetch() Spec](https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#fetch-method) and the param [Request spec](https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#request), I know the param `dispatcher` is just a feature provided by `undici`, not in the whatwg's spec.

Not sure if this change will cause new issue or worth to be supported by axios. Somehow, I think it would be more convenient if axios can provide a way to pass the second optional param to `fetch()`.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_

author: mangesh-hopstack

title: Server-Side Request Forgery Vulnerability (CVE-2024-39338)
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios is vulnerable to a Server-Side Request Forgery attack caused by unexpected behaviour where requests for path relative URLS gets processed as protocol relative URLs.

This could be leveraged by an attacker to perform arbitrary requests from the server, potentially accessing internal systems or exfiltrating sensitive data.

### To Reproduce

In this vulnerable code snippet, the developer intended to invoke a path relative to the base URL, however it allows an attacker to craft a malicious protocol-relative URL which is then requested by the server. Given protocol-relative URLs are not relevant server-side as there is no protocol to be relative to, the expected result would be an error. Instead, a valid URL is produced and requested.

Example Vulnerable Code
```typescript
const axios = require('axios');

this.axios = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'https://userapi.example.com',
});

//userId = '12345';
userId = '/google.com'

this.axios.get(`/${userId}`).then(function (response) {
  console.log(`config.baseURL:  ${response.config.baseURL}`);
  console.log(`config.method:   ${response.config.method}`);
  console.log(`config.url:      ${response.config.url}`);
  console.log(`res.responseUrl: ${response.request.res.responseUrl}`);
});
```

Expected Output (Prior to axios 1.3.2):
```typescript
(node:10243) UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: TypeError [ERR_INVALID_URL]: Invalid URL: //example.org
```

Developer might also have expected:
```yaml
config.baseURL:  https://userapi.example.com
config.method:   get
config.url:      https://userapi.example.com//www.google.com/
res.responseUrl: https://userapi.example.com//www.google.com/
```

Observed Output:
```yaml
config.baseURL:  https://userapi.example.com
config.method:   get
config.url:      //google.com
res.responseUrl: http://www.google.com/
```

This behaviour is potentially unexpected and introduces the potential for attackers to request URLs on arbitrary hosts other than the host in the base URL.

The code related to parsing and preparing the URL for server-side requests, prior to version 1.3.2, only passed one argument to the Node.js URL class.
```typescript
    const fullPath = buildFullPath(config.baseURL, config.url);
    const parsed = new URL(fullPath);
    const protocol = parsed.protocol || supportedProtocols[0];
```

Version 1.3.2 introduced `http://localhost` as a base URL for relative paths (https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5458)
```typescript
    const fullPath = buildFullPath(config.baseURL, config.url);
    const parsed = new URL(fullPath, 'http://localhost');
    const protocol = parsed.protocol || supportedProtocols[0];
```

As protocol-relative URLs are considered to be absolute, the config.baseURL value is ignored so protocol-relative URLs are passed to the URL class without a protocol. The node.js URL class will then prepend the protocol from `'http://localhost'` to the protocol-relative URL.

For example
```typescript
> new URL('//google.com', 'https://example.org');
URL {
  href: 'https://google.com/',
  ...
}

> new URL('//google.com', 'file://example.org');
URL {
  href: 'file://google.com/',
  ...
}
```

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

An error could be raised when attempting to request protocol-relative URLs server-side as unlike in a client-side browser session, there is no established protocol to be relative to.

author: paOmer

title: isURLSameOrigin fails with URL objects due to protocol mismatch
body:
### Describe the issue

When using `isURLSameOrigin` with native Javascript URL objects, the protocols mismatch as the `resolveURL` function strips the ":"-character, while the URL-object's protocol attribute does contain it. 

`isURLSameOrigin` thus returns false if used with URL objects, even if the origins match. This now leads to issues when setting `withCredentials = true` and `withXSRFToken = false` as the XSRF token will not be submitted when using URL objects.

### Example Code

```js
// locally recreated function that mimics the relevant behavior in isURLSameOrigin
const resolveURL = function (url) {
  let href = url;
  const urlParsingNode = document.createElement('a');

  urlParsingNode.setAttribute('href', href);

  return {
    href: urlParsingNode.href,
    protocol: urlParsingNode.protocol ? urlParsingNode.protocol.replace(/:$/, '') : '',
    host: urlParsingNode.host,
    search: urlParsingNode.search ? urlParsingNode.search.replace(/^\?/, '') : '',
    hash: urlParsingNode.hash ? urlParsingNode.hash.replace(/^#/, '') : '',
    hostname: urlParsingNode.hostname,
    port: urlParsingNode.port,
    pathname: (urlParsingNode.pathname.charAt(0) === '/') ?
      urlParsingNode.pathname :
      '/' + urlParsingNode.pathname
  };
};

let originURL = resolveURL("http://www.example.com");
let requestURL = new URL("http://www.example.com");
console.log(originURL.protocol);  // > http
console.log(requestURL.protocol); // > http:
```


### Expected behavior

I do not know if this is to be disregarded as no compatibility with URL objects is explicitly proclaimed in the axios docs. My personal workaround for now is to not pass URL objects directly into axios and use strings only.

A comment in `resolveURL`  however does read "urlParsingNode provides the UrlUtils interface - http://url.spec.whatwg.org/#urlutils".
According to that spec it seems to me that the ":"-character should be included (if I am reading this correctly):
"The protocol getter steps are to return [this](https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this)’s [URL](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-url)’s [scheme](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-scheme), followed by U+003A (:)." 

### Axios Version

1.7.2 / 1.x

body:
### Describe the bug

With the code provided the response interceptor doesn't run for me. The intention is to test that the error handler in the interceptor works.
I have added console.logs just before the interceptor is added and just before requests are sent, and always see that the interceptor is added before requests are sent.

```console
adding interceptor
sending request that fails
```

As seen from the console output above, neither the success nor error handler runs. Meaning the interceptor doesn't work.

Now, when I increase the timeout to 20 milliseconds, the interceptor sometimes runs and sometimes don't.
```console
adding interceptor
sending request that fails
fail
```

Another observation. On low timeout (such as 10 milliseconds), I don't see the request in the Network tab in Chrome. If I were to have a theory, it would be that browser never receives the request (properly) before it is cancelled. And that again leads to axios interceptor never running.

### To Reproduce

1. Run a request with the code below
2. If you see the "success" log, try reducing the timeout. It could be your computer is faster than the VM I am on.
3. If you don't see the "success" log and nor the "fail" log, you have reproduced the error.

### Code snippet

```js
export const axiosInstance = axios.create({
  signal: AbortSignal.timeout(10),
});

console.log('adding interceptor');
axiosInstance.interceptors.response.use(
  (response) => {
    console.log('success');
    return response;
  },
  (error) => {
    console.log('fail');
  },
```

author: jakeleventhal

title: Axios should warn about options like `baseURL` being misspelled `baseUrl`
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I wasted half an hour today trying to figure out why my base URL wasn't being respected, before I realized that I had spelled it `baseUrl` instead of `baseURL`. Axios silently ignored my parameter - given that Axios is liberal about accepting unknown options, I'm sure I'm not the first to trip over this and waste time on a silly ambiguity in the application of camelCase.

Any solutions here should also be applied to options such as `withXSRFToken`, ideally in an automated way so that any new options with ambiguous camelCase representations would be automatically included.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would love if Axios simply accepted `baseUrl` as an optional spelling of `baseURL`, since Axios is already being liberal in what it accepts, and the intent is clear.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

At the very least, I think it should log a warning - something like "baseUrl is likely a misspelling of baseURL" - to help point folks in the right direction.

let config = {

	axios.post('https://httpbin.org/post', data, config)

author: Joshuajrodrigues

title: Request for reopen #1727 Default params serialisation behaviour incompatible with recent Tomcat versions.
body:
### Describe the bug

Request for reopening the [#1727 Default params serialisation behaviour incompatible with recent Tomcat versions.](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/1727).

https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/1727#issuecomment-2244795901:

> It's unclear why it shouldn't be a default behavior - why not use standard encoding (RFC3986 / RFC1738 as qa does) as  
   expected without the need for doing workarounds?
   The problem is that one doesn't know in advance that Axios is working in such a way that might lead to unexpected bugs.

Original description
--------------------

> Summary
   Given the default params serialisation behaviour, axios out-of-the-box may fail to work with recent Tomcat versions out- 
   of-the-box.

> The problem is the way params are serialised in the absence of a custom params serialiser configuration. The code is 
   using the built-in encodeURIComponent function (good), but then proceeds to deliberately de-encode some characters. 
   In particular, it de-encodes [ and ] back to raw (problem).

> This is a problem with Tomcat because recent versions (e.g. 8.5.31+) have started being more pedantic about URI syntax, 
   and by default will reject requests that contain [ or ] in the query portion.

>  I appreciate the motive of making the URLs built by axios more human readable, and not percent-encoding characters if 
    you can get away with it. But blatant and wilful violation of RFC 3986, even if common practice for historical reasons, is a 
    problem with servers that are adopting strict-by-default behaviour.

 > Context
    axios version: v0.18.0
    Tomcat 8.5.32



reproduced on Axios 0.27.2.

title: Cannot find name 'ProgressEvent' when compiling TypeScript
body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to compile a node package I get an error:

```
node_modules/axios/index.d.ts:153:38 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'ProgressEvent'.

153   onUploadProgress?: (progressEvent: ProgressEvent) => void;
                                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~~

node_modules/axios/index.d.ts:154:40 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'ProgressEvent'.

154   onDownloadProgress?: (progressEvent: ProgressEvent) => void;
                                           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
``` 

### To Reproduce


A pure Node.js project, with the following `tsconfig.json` file：

```
{
    "compilerOptions": {
        "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
        "esModuleInterop": true,
        "lib": ["ES2017"],
        "target": "ES2017",
        "module": "commonjs",
        "outDir": "./lib",
        "declaration": true,
        "resolveJsonModule": true
    },
    "include": ["index.ts", "src/**/*.ts", "src/**/**/*.ts"],
    "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
``` 


### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

The Node.js project compiles successfully.


### Axios Version

0.28.1

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

16.19.0

### OS

MacOS 14.5

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

I reviewed the ISSUES log and discovered a similar problem reported in 2020: [#3219](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/3219), and it has been resolved in version 0.21 of axios: Fixes [#3219](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/3219).
However, in 2022, a feature-type issue requested reverting the 'any' type back to 'ProgressEvent' ([#4674](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/4674)) and initiated an MR: [#4675](https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4675). This change was ultimately merged and included in the 0.28.0 release version.
So, this issue reappeared in versions released after 0.28.0.

author: LifeActor

title: Keep-Alive behavior is different on Node.js ≤ 18 and > 18
body:
### Describe the bug

On Node.js ≤ 18 with the http adapter, Keep-Alive is off.  
On Node.js > 18 with the http adapter, Keep-Alive is on.

The [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/c6cce43cd94489f655f4488c5a50ecaf781c94f2/README.md#L575) states that Keep-Alive is always off by default.

The Node.js default behavior was changed here: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/43522

It's confusing that the same axios code works differently on different Node versions.  
I'd suggest for axios to use the same Keep-Alive behavior across different Node versions.

title: Submit a functional bug
body:
### Describe the bug

[https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/6535](url)
This is the bug report I submitted last time, and I have identified the cause, but it triggered another bug:
Related Axios versions: all

When using the 'npm start' command to run Axios based projects with preset HTTP and HTTPS proxies, this error is triggered

~~HTTP protocol packets built by Axios will be rejected by the proxy server. When starting with 'node filename', data can be sent normally~~

Further analysis has been conducted, and it is not that the proxy server refused, but rather that Axios had already refused to send out the data packet during its construction. This confirms that the issue was not caused by the API server or proxy server.

HTTP proxy is mainly used to proxy HTTP protocol, but it is not limited to processing HTTP requests.HTTP proxies can also proxy HTTPS traffic, as HTTPS establishes a tunnel on the HTTP proxy through a special HTTP method called CONNECT

### To Reproduce

_No response_

const axios = require('axios');

const url = 'https://chat.deepseek.com/api/v0/chat/completions';

title: Security Vulnerability: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Axios - CVE-2024-39338
body:
### Describe the bug

**Body:**

Hello Axios Team,

I am reporting a high-severity security vulnerability related to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Axios, identified as CVE-2024-39338. This vulnerability affects Axios versions 1.3.2 through 1.7.3, where path-relative URLs are incorrectly processed as protocol-relative URLs, leading to potential SSRF attacks.

**Why Reporting Here:**

I attempted to report this issue through your designated Huntr platform, as outlined in the SECURITY.md file. However, it appears that Axios is no longer listed or active on Huntr, which led me to report the issue directly through GitHub.

**Vulnerability Details:**

	•	`CVE: CVE-2024-39338`
	•	Affected Versions: Axios `>= 1.3.2`, `<= 1.7.3`
	•	Description: The vulnerability allows for SSRF via unexpected behavior where path-relative URLs are treated as protocol-relative URLs, potentially leading to exposure to external and internal services.
	•	Impact: This vulnerability can be exploited to make unauthorized requests, potentially accessing sensitive data or systems.

I strongly recommend prioritizing the investigation and patching of this issue, as it poses a significant risk to applications using the affected versions of Axios.

Please feel free to contact me if further details are required or if I can assist in any way.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Best regards,
Hatem Soliman

title: `status` field missing from `AxiosError`
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- Please, ensure your issue is not related to CORS or Mixed Content Issue (only relevant for browsers)
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- If you need a new feature there's a chance it's already implemented in a [library](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/ECOSYSTEM.md) or you can implement it using [interceptors](https://github.com/axios/axios#interceptors).
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

**⚠️👆 Delete the instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️**

-->

#### Summary

In the [Javascript implementation of `AxiosError`](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/core/AxiosError.js), the `status` is only attached to the `toJson`, but not in the `AxiosError` itself. However, in the [type declaration of `AxiosError`](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/4f99675fe7eae503422f932b9407242dbc1b7d55/index.d.ts#L416), there is `status?: number` field. Because of this, I have accidentally misused it for quite a while, as it is more obvious and direct than `error.response.status`.

The PR https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/5331 seems to already fix this issue, and it was even approved. But it was not merged. Can axios team merge this, or there is something else that the PR need to resolve?

title: Request.credentials breaks Cloudflare Workers since #6505
body:
### Describe the bug

#6505 addressed an issue with the fetch adapter, where it incorrectly set `withCredentials` instead of `credentials` in the `Request` object, leading to unexpected behavior.

However, fixing this issue now causes Cloudflare Workers' workerd runtime to throw `Error: The 'credentials' field on 'RequestInitializerDict' is not implemented.` as it does not support the `credentials` property (https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-sdk/issues/2514)

### To Reproduce

Setup Axios with the fetch adapter on a Cloudflare Worker. Any request will throw.

Removing the `credentials` property in the `Request` object fixes this.
https://github.com/prianyu/axios/blob/d4ea8fb23c8fbca1bac855f81c86469c9b975e70/lib/adapters/fetch.js#L168

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

While I understand this is perhaps more of a Cloudflare issue than an Axios one, I hope that a solution could be implemented so we don't have to patch this manually; this wouldn't necessarily require exporting a specific build for Cloudflare Workers or adding runtime-specific checks.

A solution to this might be to change the current behavior, stop setting `credentials` to `omit` when it is not set by the user and leave it undefined. The Fetch API would the fall back to the default behavior, which is `same-origin` and not `omit` (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API/Using_Fetch#including_credentials)

Or, there could be a way to override `Request` properties set by Axios or set the `credentials` property to undefined if needed.

### Axios Version

1.7.4

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

author: filoucrackeur

title: Streaming on Axios with Fetch-adapter not working on Safari
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello everyone,

a colleague of mine recently stumbled upon a bug, while using Axios on a Safari. It turns out, streaming with Axios and the fetch adapter is not possible on Safari.

I did some research in our code and found out, that the bug was introduced after switching from the native fetch library to Axios with fetch adapter. Nobody noticed it until recently, as it was working perfectly on Firefox and Chrome (even on MacOS).
After another bit of research, I could narrow it down to the ReadableStream response, that Axios is providing. See the attached CodeSandBox example for further details.

Why do I write it here, although it might be a bug related to the underlying Safari?
For the sake of documentation and for the sake of preventing others to fall into the same pit. And, perhaps, there is a possibility to mitigate the problem by handling the user agent in Axios directly (see workaround bit further below)?
A somewhat familiar (perhaps, the same?) issue was already reported on StackOverflow: https://stackoverflow.com/q/78713060

To anyone experiencing the same issue: There is a workaround. Detect the user agent and check for Safari. For Safari, instead of using Axios, use the native fetch library - just in case, you want to stick to Axios (like we did in our project)
For some inspiration, I created another CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/ecstatic-grass-6skn7l?workspaceId=18337018-7dab-4311-9114-b5cf242336fd
It contains a browser-check using bowser-js, based on the CodeSandbox used in the "To reproduce" section. If you click on the button in the preview, it will now use Axios for the calls for the non-Safari browsers or fallback to the native fetch library for Safari.

### To Reproduce

I wrote a small example in CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/objective-gwen-p8jlgv?workspaceId=18337018-7dab-4311-9114-b5cf242336fd

Simply click on the button reading "Click me!" in the preview to trigger the request.

Here is a screenshot of the error:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2fd5d022-323d-4db7-a335-8b65570c5c65)
Notice, how line 13 in AxiosCaller is the "culprit". The line merely consists of the `await reader.read()`, as you can see.

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

No error is thrown, if I access an endpoint serving a streamed response using Axios.

### Axios Version

1.7.4 (already experienced with 1.7.2+)

### Adapter Version

Fetch

17.5 (18618.2.12.111.5, 18618)

author: HiveMe

title: Breaking change in Axios 1.7.4 regarding default host in HTTP adapter
body:
### Describe the bug

This commit:
https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/6b6b605eaf73852fb2dae033f1e786155959de3a#diff-586c04c24608b86a074073c5514cc195be5c4c87ddb56da233f6d998ada4159f 
changed the code from
`const parsed = new URL(fullPath, 'http://localhost');`
to
`const parsed = new URL(fullPath, utils.hasBrowserEnv ? platform.origin : undefined);`.

This can generate an "Invalid URL" error in the URL constructor, since it expects the host to be specified.

### To Reproduce

**Please run "npm install" before trying these;** it seems that codesandbox sometimes shows up with an outdated node_modules folder?!

Not working with:
* 1.7.5: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/sharp-dawn-3c7qk4

```
Node version: v18.20.4
Axios version: 1.7.5
node:internal/process/esm_loader:40
      internalBinding('errors').triggerUncaughtException(
                                ^
TypeError [ERR_INVALID_URL]: Invalid URL
```

Working fine with:
* 1.7.3: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/objective-booth-98649r

```
Node version: v18.20.4
Axios version: 1.7.3
404
```

### Code snippet

```ts
const instance = new Axios({});
console.log((await instance.get('/relative')).status);
// Axios 1.7.3:  Calls http://localhost/relative
// Axios 1.7.4+: Throws "Invalid URL" from URL constructor
```

### Expected behavior

Would be be acceptable to change the code slightly like this?
```diff
- const parsed = new URL(fullPath, utils.hasBrowserEnv ? platform.origin : undefined);
+ const parsed = new URL(fullPath, utils.hasBrowserEnv ? platform.origin : 'http://localhost');
```

Then it would not be a breaking change for Node environments.
My understanding is that this wouldn't affect the CVE fix, but maybe I'm wrong.

I understand that the fallback to "localhost" is questionable, but still, removing it is a breaking change, so I suggest to delay it until version 2.0.
Your readme contains a section "Semver", which sounds like you would be using semantic versioning.

### Axios Version

1.7.4 and 1.7.5 - No longer working.
1.7.3 - Working,

### Adapter Version

HTTP

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

18.20.4, 20.16.0

### OS

Ubuntu 20.04

_No response_

title: Breaking change in Axios 0.28.0 regarding default proxy
body:
### Describe the bug

In Axios version 0.28.0, a breaking change was introduced with the addition of the `proxy-from-env` package. This npm package reads the proxy address from the `npm_config_proxy` environment variable. Since our company’s internal network already sets this environment variable, upgrading to 0.28.0 causes Axios to use this proxy, which leads to network connectivity issues and request timeouts. 

This is not the expected behavior, we want Axios to operate without using any proxy. The current workaround is to revert to version 0.27.x.

Related to https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4436

### To Reproduce

1. Upgrade Axios to version 0.28.x.
2. Ensure that the `npm_config_proxy` environment variable is set.
3. Make an Axios request.

_No response_

title: CommonJS / ESM mixup when using a lib that has Axios has peer dependency
body:
### Describe the issue

I am authoring a library that has Axios 1.7.4 as peer dependency (so Axios is not bundled with it).
The lib has both ESModule and CommonJS output.

When I import both the lib and Axios in a React application (CRA or webpack), I have this error when opening the app in the browser:
```
index.mjs:315 Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: axios__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_0__.create is not a function
    at BaseService.axiosInstance (index.mjs:315:1)
    at ./node_modules/.pnpm/mylib@0.29.0-alpha.8_@types+react@18.0.28_axios@1.7.4_@mui+system_7n6tjqsrmbejf4ko57dpnqh2ey/node_modules/my-lib/dist/index.mjs (index.mjs:324:1)
    at options.factory (react refresh:6:1)
    at __webpack_require__ (bootstrap:24:1)
    at sharing:44:1
    at __webpack_require__.m.<computed> (consumes:186:1)
    at options.factory (react refresh:6:1)
    at __webpack_require__ (bootstrap:24:1)
    at fn (hot module replacement:62:1)
    at ./src/bootstrap.tsx (src_bootstrap_tsx.chunk.js:16:68)
```
I can see in the debugger that the CommonJS version of Axios is loaded (the one with a named `default` export):
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1306ba8-ee7e-4250-99dd-c4e14e78e280)

Any clue on how to solve this problem?

FYI: if I dont use Axios as a peer dependency, I dont have the issue when consuming the lib (the ESM version of Axios is used):
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c022f52-a910-46e8-bafd-0696f74c5efe)



### Example Code

```js
// snippet of index.mjs in mylib
import axios from "axios";
// ...
var BaseService = class {
  headers = {};
  constructor(headers) {
    this.headers = { ...headers };
  }
  axiosInstance({ baseUrl = API_BASE_URL, omitResponseInterceptors } = {}) {
    const api = axios.create({ baseURL: baseUrl });
    api.interceptors.request.use((config) => requestInterceptor(this.headers, config), requestErrorResponse);
    if (!omitResponseInterceptors) {
      api.interceptors.response.use((res) => res, responseErrorInterceptor);
    }
    return api;
  }
};
```


### Expected behavior

I'm expecting the consuming app to use either ESM of my lib & axios, or both CommonJS versions.

Im not sure if it is an issue with my lib, axios, webpack or a more general issues when consuming modules that publish both CJS & ESM inside a CommonJS app... 

Thanks for the guidance 🙏

here's my axios api code:

```
import axios, { type AxiosError } from "axios";

const headers: Readonly<Record<string, string | boolean>> = {
  Accept: "application/json",
  "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
  "Access-Control-Allow-Credentials": true,
};

async function handleError(error: AxiosError<never>): Promise<never> {
  return await Promise.reject(error);
}

const api = axios.create({
  headers,
  baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_URL!,
});

api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => config,
  async (error) => await Promise.reject(error),
);

api.interceptors.response.use(
  (response) => response,
  async (error) => await handleError(error),
);

export { api };

title: 🏁 RoSctober 2024: The Open Source Developer Challenge is about to start
body:
As part of the RoSctober Race presented by [Race of Sloths](https://race-of-sloths.com/), we recognized this repo to be highly valuable in the open-source movement. Existing and new contributors will be rewarded proportionally to their contributions to the selected open-source projects. Read below for the official rules, and let’s see who will climb to the top of the Sloth Hall of Fame! 🦥 ## TLDR for maintainers: - This issue is an invitation to join [Race of Sloths](https://race-of-sloths.com/) project - To accept the invitation, comment `@race-of-sloths accept` - Once the invitation is accepted, your contributors are eligible to get a share of $10,000 prize pool with their contributions to your project in October; they will just need to mention @race-of-sloths in their PR ## What is the RoSctober Race and Race of Sloths? Our goal is to make the open-source contributions more fun and rewarding. We hope that for some contributors it will bring joy through participation in weekly and monthly quests, and boost their profiles by earning points and competing in the leaderboards - all in a non-intrusive way with just a couple of commands directly in GitHub interface. 1. Your contributors are welcome to participate within your normal contribution flow 2. RoSctober Prize Pool: $10,000 USDC 3. Eligibility: Earn at least 100 SP during RoSctober to qualify for the prize 4. Competition Timeline: October 1st to October 31st, 2024 5. Submission Process for contributors: Tag @race-of-sloths in any comment on GitHub to automatically submit your PR 6. Scoring system: Tag @race-of-sloths score [1/2/3/5/8/13] (for maintainers only!). Detailed info here: [Sloth Points System](https://race-of-sloths.com/faq)
More information

- [Full rule set](https://github.com/NEAR-DevHub/race-of-sloths/wiki/RoSctober-Official-Rules) - [Race of Sloths project](https://race-of-sloths.com/) - [X](https://x.com/race_of_sloths) - [Telegram community](https://t.me/race_of_sloths)

Bot commands - ➕ **Include a PR:** `@race-of-sloths include` to enter the Race with your PR - 👋 **Invite a PR author:** `@race-of-sloths invite` to invite the author who haven't tagged the bot in the Race - 🛑 **Reject this PR:** `@race-of-sloths exclude` to send the PR back to the drawing board. - ⭐ **Assign points:** `@race-of-sloths score [1/2/3/5/8/13]` to award points based on your assessment. - 🔕 **Pause us:** `@race-of-sloths pause` to stop the bot on your repo.

author: anytizer

title: AxiosHeaders doesn't handle multiple headers returned via fetch adapter
body:
### Describe the bug

When using fetch adapter in node.js, axios iterates over headers by using `header.entries()` (https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/c96aa6257f5a060fedac27ae424486acd3ee705f/lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js#L104).

Headers from fetch will be in this format:
```
[
  [
    "access-control-allow-credentials",
    "true"
  ],
  [
    "access-control-allow-origin",
    ""
  ],
  [
    "connection",
    "keep-alive"
  ],
  [
    "content-length",
    "1991"
  ],
  [
    "content-type",
    "application/json; charset=utf-8"
  ],
  [
    "date",
    "Tue, 01 Oct 2024 15:49:38 GMT"
  ],
  [
    "keep-alive",
    "timeout=5"
  ],
  [
    "set-cookie",
    "test-name1=test-token1; path=/; httponly"
  ],
  [
    "set-cookie",
    "test-name2=test-token2; path=/; httponly"
  ],
  [
    "vary",
    "Origin, Accept-Encoding"
  ]
]
```

Note that there are 2 cookies set. However, axios will overwrite the first cookie with the second one.

I presume it's expecting to get cookies in an array form. But fetch adapter returns a raw list.

### To Reproduce

https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/optimistic-worker-mx8tcx?file=%2Findex.js

My output:
```
with http adapter [ 'a=x; Path=/', 'b=y; Path=/' ]
with fetch adapter b=y; Path=/
```

### Code snippet

```js
import axiosLib from "axios";

const axios1 = axiosLib.create({adapter: 'http', maxRedirects: 0, validateStatus: () => true}); 
const resp1 = await axios1.get("http://httpbin.org/cookies/set?a=x&b=y");
console.log('with http adapter', resp1.headers['set-cookie']);

const axios2 = axiosLib.create({adapter: 'fetch', maxRedirects: 0, validateStatus: () => true}); 
const resp2 = await axios2.get("http://httpbin.org/cookies/set?a=x&b=y", {
    fetchOptions: {
        redirect: 'manual'
    }
});
console.log('with fetch adapter', resp2.headers['set-cookie']);
```


### Expected behavior

Fetch adapter should reorganize headers so they are in node.js format, where repeated headers are collected.

### Axios Version

1.7.7

### Adapter Version

bundled fetch

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

v20.12.1

### OS

linux

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_

title: Axios mishandles HTTP 204 response: attempts to parse empty body, causing an error
body:
### Describe the bug

When making an API call that results in a 204 No Content response, axios incorrectly tries to parse the response body, leading to an error.

According to the HTTP spec, a 204 response should not contain a body. However, axios seems to expect a body and throws an error when the response is empty.

### To Reproduce

1.	Make a GET request using axios to an API endpoint that returns a 204 No Content response.
2.	Observe that axios attempts to parse the response body even though it is empty.

### Code snippet

```js
Version:
axios version 1.7.7

Environment:

• Node.js version: 18.x
• OS: macOS 13.5
```


### Expected behavior

axios should handle 204 No Content responses without attempting to parse the body.

### Axios Version

1.7.7

### Adapter Version

Default

### Browser

Chrome

### Browser Version

118.0.5993.88

### Node.js Version

v18.17.1

### OS

macOS 13.5

- Axios version: 1.7.7
- Adapter: Default (Node.js `http` adapter)

author: devlohani99

title: Why return the string and not the JS object if we know that the responseType is JSON?
body:
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/6841d8d18ddc71cc1bd202ffcfddb3f95622eef3/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L48

In this line, for JSON-type responses, it's returning a string. This isn't a problem because Axios' default [transformResponse](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/defaults/index.js#L100) includes a JSON.parse.

However, when implementing a custom transformResponse, I was encountering issues handling the request response. That's when I realized it's because my custom transformResponse doesn't have a JSON.parse.

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

title: axios.post ignore proxy
body:
### Describe the bug

When using axios methods other than `get` with proxy defined axios ignore proxy, but if specify method as an option proxy is applied.
This is going to be ignored:
```
axios.post('http://example.com', {
    proxy: {
         protocol: 'http',
         host: <host>,
         port: <port>,
    }
}
```
This will work as expected:
```
axios('http://example.com', {
    method: 'POST",
    proxy: {
         protocol: 'http',
         host: <host>,
         port: <port>,
    }
}
```

axios.post('http://example.com', {

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Some frameworks provide `fetch`-compatible APIs for handling HTTP requests in specific contexts, e.g. [SvelteKit](https://svelte.dev/docs/kit/web-standards#Fetch-APIs) for internal requests in an SSR context, and [Tauri](https://tauri.app/plugin/http-client/) for CORS-less requests in a webview context.

### Describe the solution you'd like

The `fetch` adapter for `axios` would allow passing a custom `fetch` function, e.g. provided by the above-mentioned frameworks :

```js
const client = axios.create({
    adapter: {
        fetch: customFetch
    }
});
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Some third-party adapters have this feature but struggle to accurately reproduce the behavior of official `axios` adapters.

author: elliottkember

title: Axios standardizes headers to be case-insensitive, which causes issues for case-sensitive APIs
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

I have a question regarding the capitalization of headers in Axios requests. By default, it seems that Axios capitalizes header names (e.g., "content-type" becomes "Content-Type" in some versions).

This behavior can cause issues when working with case-sensitive APIs, such as AWS API Gateway, which require headers to follow specific casing ([as noted in the AWS documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-known-issues.html#:~:text=Header%20names%20and%20query%20parameters,back%20by%20your%20integration%20endpoint)).

I understand that this decision may follow [RFC 2616](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec4.html#sec4.2), which states that HTTP header names are case-insensitive. However, this approach can make Axios challenging to use in environments where case-sensitive headers are required.

I’ve noticed other issues opened on this topic, but they have been closed without a final solution, with the decision being to follow the specification:

Issue https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/4509
Issue https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/413

If there are other related issues, I couldn’t find them here.

Is there any way to configure Axios to send headers in a case-sensitive manner, or is there a workaround to address this issue? Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

### To Reproduce

I encountered the error while using AWS pre-signed URLs. The backend of my system generates this upload URL, and the frontend sends the file to the obtained endpoint. My API is written in .NET 8.

### Code snippet
This is an example of a manual PUT request without Axios, where headers are set to be case-sensitive:

```js

PUT /programa_29151_capitulo_9.fdx?AWSAccessKeyId=*****&Expires=1730412840&x-amz-security-token=*****&Signature=***** HTTP/1.1
accept: application/json
content-type: 
```

In comparison, here’s the same PUT request using Axios:

```js
PUT /programa_11111_capitulo_99.fdx?AWSAccessKeyId=*****&Expires=1730412968&x-amz-security-token=*****&Signature=***** HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
Content-Type: 
```

The only difference between these two requests is the capitalization of the content-type and accept headers, with the manual request having these headers in lowercase (content-type and accept), while Axios automatically capitalizes them (Content-Type and Accept). This is a real example I’ve encountered; the first request succeeds, but the second returns a 403 Forbidden error. I’ve hidden the other request details as they are exactly the same.

### Axios Version

1.7.7

### Node.js Version

22.2.0

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
React 18.2.0
```

Thank you!

title: File upload w/ fetch adapter - ERR_H2_OR_QUIC_REQUIRED
body:
### Describe the bug

On localhost running NextJs 14 app, I get `ERR_H2_OR_QUIC_REQUIRED` network error when trying to upload a file using `axios` w/ `fetch` adapter. When I do so with `fetch` without axios, request passes w/o issues.

### To Reproduce

```
axios.defaults.adapter = 'fetch' as const

const formData = new FormData();
formData.append('file', file)  // file is the standard File type which extends Blob

axios({
  url: '...',
  method: 'post', 
  headers: {'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data', },  // can be left out, but does not change a thing
  data: formData,
})
```

results in (copied from Network panel in DevTools), e.g.
```
fetch('...', {
  "headers": {
    "accept": "application/json, text/plain, */*",    
    "content-type": "multipart/form-data; boundary=----WebKitFormBoundarykJp6QP5PWw3dxOui",
    "sec-ch-ua": "\"Chromium\";v=\"130\", \"Google Chrome\";v=\"130\", \"Not?A_Brand\";v=\"99\"",
    "sec-ch-ua-mobile": "?0",
    "sec-ch-ua-platform": "\"Windows\""
  },
  "referrer": "http://localhost:3000/projects/602/files",
  "referrerPolicy": "strict-origin-when-cross-origin",
  "body": null,
  "method": "POST",
  "mode": "cors",
  "credentials": "include"
});
```

and causing `ERR_H2_OR_QUIC_REQUIRED` HTTP error as I'm not using HTTP/2 nor QUIC on my localhost.

However, when I do `fetch` request w/o axios:
```
fetch('...', {
  method: 'post', 
  body: formData,
})
```
request ends up being, e.g.:
```
fetch("...", {
  "headers": {
    "accept": "*/*",
    "accept-language": "en,en-US;q=0.9,cs;q=0.8,sk;q=0.7",    
    "cache-control": "no-cache",
    "content-type": "multipart/form-data; boundary=----WebKitFormBoundaryigwKrAUJ5zJxSkIg",
    "pragma": "no-cache",
    "sec-ch-ua": "\"Chromium\";v=\"130\", \"Google Chrome\";v=\"130\", \"Not?A_Brand\";v=\"99\"",
    "sec-ch-ua-mobile": "?0",
    "sec-ch-ua-platform": "\"Windows\"",
    "sec-fetch-dest": "empty",
    "sec-fetch-mode": "cors",
    "sec-fetch-site": "same-origin"
  },
  "referrer": "http://localhost:3000/projects/602/files",
  "referrerPolicy": "strict-origin-when-cross-origin",
  "body": "------WebKitFormBoundaryigwKrAUJ5zJxSkIg\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"projectId\"\r\n\r\n602\r\n------WebKitFormBoundaryigwKrAUJ5zJxSkIg\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"folderId\"\r\n\r\n0\r\n------WebKitFormBoundaryigwKrAUJ5zJxSkIg\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"empty.csv\"\r\nContent-Type: text/csv\r\n\r\n\r\n------WebKitFormBoundaryigwKrAUJ5zJxSkIg--\r\n",
  "method": "POST",
  "mode": "cors",
  "credentials": "include"
});
```
and passing without any network error.

title: Different error handling behavior when validateStatus is set to undefined vs. not passed at all
body:
### Describe the bug

When setting `validateStatus` to `undefined`, we get a different behavior than not setting it all

This happens in situation like wrapping axios in another invocation function, and getting default values. This behavior seems unexpected and not documented (as far as I can see), and may cause error handling to be completely disabled.




### To Reproduce

Execute an axios request with `validateStatus:undefined` and without it, to a url that returns an error status code.

### Code snippet

```js
axios({ url: "bla", validateStatus: undefined }) // no error thrown on status codes
```
```
axios({ url: "bla" }) // error is thrown on status codes
```
```


### Expected behavior

Some options could be to either:
1. Default to original behavior when `undefined` (to disable error checking use `validateStatus: () => true`)
2. OR - Document this behavior (in tsdoc + https://axios-http.com/docs/handling_errors)

title: A problem with setting AxiosError.prototype.toJSON
body:
### Describe the bug

`util.inherits` fails on `Object.assign(props)` when `Error.prototype.toJSON`  is already defined as a non-writable. 

We're using a library that adds a toJSON to the prototype of Error.
It does it using something in the spirit of:
```
Object.defineProperty(Error.prorotype, 'toJSON', { configurable: true, writable: false, enumerable: false, value() {
  /* some customization code  that makes sure name, stack and any own property passes */ 
})
```
Then, `axios/lib/util.js:inerit` fails on `Object.assign`.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4147783-fe1c-4ed4-a2eb-bd4919cae0a8)

Also - see reproduction below.


### To Reproduce

Case isolated in the code snippet below.

### Code snippet

```js
Object.defineProperty(Error.prototype, 'toJSON', {
  configurable: true,
  writable: false,
  enumerable: false,
  value: function foo() { return {} }
})

const utils = (await import(path.join(process.cwd(), 'node_modules/axios/lib/utils.js'))).default

function MyError() { }

//This does not work (throws):
utils.inherits(MyError, Error, { toJSON() { return {} } }) //<-- throws: TypeError: Cannot assign to read only property 'toJSON' of object 'Error'

//This does not throw, and works as expected:
const util = require('node:util')
util.inherits(MyError, Error, { toJSON() { return {} } })
```


### Expected behavior

- Should not throw
- Should set the toJSON on the extended prototype

### Axios Version

1.7.7

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

v20.18.0

### OS

Fedora in WSL.
Reproduced on my Win 11 also with node 20.12.2

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

_No response_

title: Type errors with TypeScript 5.6.3
body:
### Describe the bug

We've update our project's TypeScript version to `5.6.3` (`latest`) and started getting some type errors for axios (`1.7.7`)

```
[0]       TS2345: Argument of type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosInstance' is not assignable to parameter of type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosInstance'.
[0]   Types of property 'defaults' are incompatible.
[0]     Type 'Omit<import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosDefaults<any>, "headers"> & { headers: import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").HeadersDefaults & { ...; }; }' is not assignable to type 'Omit<import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosDefaults<any>, "headers"> & { headers: import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").HeadersDefaults & { ...; }; }'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]       Type 'Omit<AxiosDefaults<any>, "headers"> & { headers: HeadersDefaults & { [key: string]: AxiosHeaderValue; }; }' is not assignable to type 'Omit<AxiosDefaults<any>, "headers">'.
[0]         Types of property 'transformRequest' are incompatible.
[0]           Type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer | import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer[] | undefined' is not assignable to type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer | import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer[] | undefined'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]             Type 'AxiosRequestTransformer' is not assignable to type 'AxiosRequestTransformer | AxiosRequestTransformer[] | undefined'.
[0]               Type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer' is not assignable to type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestTransformer'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]                 The 'this' types of each signature are incompatible.
[0]                   Type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").InternalAxiosRequestConfig<any>' is not assignable to type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").InternalAxiosRequestConfig<any>'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]                     Types of property 'headers' are incompatible.
[0]                       Type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestHeaders' is not assignable to type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosRequestHeaders'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]                         Type 'AxiosRequestHeaders' is not assignable to type 'Partial<RawAxiosHeaders & { Accept: AxiosHeaderValue; "Content-Length": AxiosHeaderValue; "User-Agent": AxiosHeaderValue; "Content-Encoding": AxiosHeaderValue; Authorization: AxiosHeaderValue; } & { ...; }>'.
[0]                           Types of property 'Accept' are incompatible.
[0]                             Type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosHeaderValue | undefined' is not assignable to type 'import("/path/to/project/node_modules/axios/index").AxiosHeaderValue | undefined'. Two different types with this name exist, but they are unrelated.
[0]                               Type 'AxiosHeaders' is not assignable to type 'AxiosHeaderValue | undefined'.
[0]                                 Type 'AxiosHeaders' is missing the following properties from type 'string[]': length, pop, push, join, and 33 more.
```

Adding `// @ts-ignore` makes the error go away and our app works normally, but this is not really a solution.
We already tried suggestions described in following issue pages, but no luck so far.
https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5034
https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5476
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75267700/type-is-not-assignable-to-type-axiosrequestheaders

**Note: with TypeScript `5.5.4` those errors do not occur!**

### To Reproduce

Our setup is actually pretty simple:
1- A base API

```
export declare class BaseAPI {
    protected basePath: string;
    protected axios: AxiosInstance;
    constructor(basePath?: string, axios?: AxiosInstance);
}
```

2- A class extending the base API
```
export declare class ControllerAPI extends BaseAPI {
    submit(id: string, options?: AxiosRequestConfig): Promise<AxiosResponse<void>>;
}
```

3- Try to use it as follows, which gives us the errors above.

```
 const client = new ControllerAPI(
    undefined,
    axios
  );
```

body:
### Describe the bug

When using an URIencoded string in my url, Axios automatically decodes the slashes.

### To Reproduce

See code snippet

### Code snippet

    console.log(response);

  .catch(function (error) {

```

title: Fetch adapter works with different HTTP responses
body:
### Describe the issue

In the latest version of axios, fetch adapter is already supported. What I want to discuss here is a scenario. I looked at the source code of fetch adapter. It determines which method to call on `Response` to get data based on the `responseType` passed in by the user.

So, if I have an api, `/api/exp`, it normally returns a `200` HTTP status code and `json` data as response's data. But in some abnormal situations, it may return a `500` HTTP status code and an `internal server error` HTML as response's data. At this time, if `res.json()` is called on the `Response` object based on the `responseType` being json, an error will be reported, prompting `SyntaxError`, and then going to `AxiosError`. This error has no response object, that is, the user cannot perceive the `500` error, but can only perceive that the request has an error.

I want to discuss what best practices are there in this scenario, or whether this is an expected behavior that requires the user to bear the judgment of the mind.

title: 1.7.8. breaks typescript type generation
body:
### Describe the bug

We generate some client code with OpenAPI Generator and generate types for it calling `tsc` 
The type definition generated prior 1.7.8. looks like
`someAPIMethod(requestParameters: SomeRequestParameters, options?: RequestOptions): Promise<import("axios").AxiosResponse<void, any>>;`
when we change to 1.7.8 it becomes:
`someAPIMethod(requestParameters: SomeRequestParameters, options?: RequestOptions): Promise<import("axios", { with: { "resolution-mode": "require" } }).AxiosResponse<void, any>>;`
Which no longer builds.
I think the culprit is [This commit](https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/c71811b00f2fcff558e4382ba913bdac4ad7200e)
Typescript recognizes the import from the commonjs types and thinks that the module is commonjs and adds the resolution-mode hint.
I don't think it is correct to import from cjs inside the (ESM)ts types.

title: Node AggregateError is being returned as ETIMEDOUT with no message
body:
### Describe the bug

We have an intermittent issue on a service making external HTTPS API calls.
The actual issue isn't particularly relevant, but our problem is that Axios swallowed most of the underlying AggregateError making diagnosis hard.

Node was throwing an AggregateError combining a number of ETIMEDOUT and EHOSTUNREACH errors, contained in a `.errors` collection - but the parent AggregateError object had a code `ETIMEDOUT` (Node just uses the code of the first nested error), a _blank_ message, and a name `AggregateError`

We were logging just the message and the code (as our payloads have sensitive data so we avoided logging the whole Error object - it contains the payload!) and this made it quite difficult to work out that this was more than just a time out.

Eventually I managed to get a stack trace which made things clearer:

```text
AggregateError
    at AxiosError.from (file:///workspace/node_modules/axios/lib/core/AxiosError.js:92:14)
    at RedirectableRequest.handleRequestError (file:///workspace/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:620:25)
    at RedirectableRequest.emit (node:events:530:35)
    at RedirectableRequest.emit (node:domain:489:12)
    at eventHandlers.<computed> (/workspace/node_modules/follow-redirects/index.js:49:24)
    at ClientRequest.emit (node:events:518:28)
    at ClientRequest.emit (node:domain:489:12)
    at emitErrorEvent (node:_http_client:101:11)
    at TLSSocket.socketErrorListener (node:_http_client:504:5)
    at TLSSocket.emit (node:events:518:28)
    at Axios.request (file:///workspace/node_modules/axios/lib/core/Axios.js:45:41)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at async requestAuthToken (file:///workspace/dist/src/auth-endpoint-handler.js:58:12)
    at async file:///workspace/dist/src/auth-endpoint-handler.js:25:27
```

(if it helps we are using Axios 1.7.7 on NodeJS 20.18)

### To Reproduce

_No response_

_No response_

### Expected behavior

It'd be good to have an `Error.message` indicating that the problem is an AggregateError - or more generally, if wrapping an Error that has no message, setting a message to the name of the underlying error (or some other informative message)

author: thewh1teagle

title: Add Support for Proxy Tunneling
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Axios currently does not have native support for proxy tunneling, particularly for HTTPS requests. This limitation makes it challenging to route requests through proxies in environments that require tunneling, especially when dealing with secure servers, authenticated proxies, and complex redirect scenarios.

Proxy tunneling is critical for:

Routing HTTP and HTTPS traffic through corporate proxies.
Supporting authentication and environmental configurations (e.g., http_proxy, https_proxy, and no_proxy environment variables).
Handling redirects and secure connections in proxy scenarios.

### Describe the solution you'd like

We propose implementing native support for proxy tunneling in Axios, leveraging the widely used http-proxy-agent library. This solution has been designed to handle a variety of use cases, ensuring robust and flexible functionality.

The following cases are supported by the solution:

- Supports proxies with and without authentication
- Respects HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables
- Supports both HTTP and HTTPS servers
- Supports both HTTP and HTTPS proxies
- Handles various redirect types
- Supports exclusions via NO_PROXY environment variable

Benefits:

- Eliminates the need for external workarounds by directly integrating proxy tunneling into Axios.
- Simplifies proxy configurations for Axios users, especially in enterprise use cases.
- Enhances Axios's capabilities for routing secure traffic through proxies, making it a more versatile HTTP client.
- Handles complex scenarios, including secure proxy-server combinations and advanced redirect behaviors.

Before proceeding with a pull request, we would like feedback from the maintainers on:
- Alignment of this feature with Axios's roadmap.
- Any additional use cases or concerns to consider during implementation.

We have a solution ready that supports the described use cases using http-proxy-agent and are eager to contribute to the project.

Branch with changes : https://github.com/armandoqf/axios/tree/feature/proxying-https-requests

Thank you for your time and consideration!

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

author: susanne99

title: Add generic type to response headers
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Sometimes APIs use response headers to communicate important metadata, such as rate limit information. At the moment there is no way to type what response headers we expect for a given request, which makes it hard to express/document this.

My use-case is primarily around code generation from openapi specifications, where I'd like to be able to include the declared response headers in the generated code, eg:
```yaml
          headers:
            X-RateLimit-Limit:
              "$ref": "#/components/headers/x-rate-limit-limit"
            X-RateLimit-Remaining:
              "$ref": "#/components/headers/x-rate-limit-remaining"
            X-RateLimit-Reset:
              "$ref": "#/components/headers/x-rate-limit-reset"
```
Should be expressed in output client functions like: https://github.com/mnahkies/openapi-code-generator/blob/main/integration-tests/typescript-axios/src/generated/api.github.com.yaml/client.ts#L1898-L1946

### Describe the solution you'd like

A new generic parameter added to the `AxiosResponse` and associated header types that could be used similar to this:
```typescript
type ResponseBody = {...}

type ResponseHeaders = {
  "x-rate-limit-remaining": string
  "x-rate-limit-used": string
}

function getSomeResource(): Promise<AxiosResponse<ResponseBodyType, ResponseHeaders>> {
...
}
```

Unfortunately there is already a second generic parameter on the `AxiosResponse` so care would be needed to do this in a way that was non-breaking.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I can probably workaround this on my side, either by moving away from the `AxiosResponse` type, or through an intersection type or similar, but it would be nice if `axios` made it easier to express this kind of information out of the box.

### Additional context/Screenshots

There are a couple of semi-related issues:
- https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5967
- https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/6677

That would also be nice to see resolved.

title: Infinite loop in Axios response interceptor when rejecting an error
body:
### Describe the issue

When an API request fails (e.g., a 401 error), the error is properly intercepted in the Axios response interceptor. However, the Promise.reject() call within the interceptor causes the error to re-enter the interceptor itself. This results in an infinite loop where the error is processed repeatedly in the interceptor.

### Example Code

```js
api.interceptors.response.use(
    (response) => response,
    (error) => {
        if (error.response) {
            const errData = error.response.data;
            return Promise.reject({
                code: errData.error.code,
                type: errData.error.type,
                detail: errData.error.detail,
            });
        } 
    }
);
```


### Expected behavior

Expected Behavior:
The error should pass through the interceptor once, and the Promise.reject() should propagate the error to the calling function's catch block, without re-triggering the interceptor.

Actual Behavior:
The error enters the interceptor multiple times, causing an infinite loop. In some cases, the error.response becomes undefined in subsequent iterations, leading to unexpected behavior.

Possible Cause:
The Promise.reject() call in the interceptor is re-captured by the interceptor itself, creating a loop.

### Axios Version

1.7.9

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

Chrome

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

20.8.1

_No response_

```TYPESCRIPT
    const response = await fetch(urlString, {
      method: "PATCH",
      headers: {
        Accept: "application/json",
        "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      },
      body: JSON.stringify(tuneUpdateData),
    });
```

The tuneUpdataData is:
```JSON
tuneUpdateDataStringified: {
  "title": "Sí Bheag, Sí Mhór",
  "type": "air/waltz",
  "structure": "AABB",
  "mode": "D Major",
  "incipit": "de|:f3e d2|d2 de d2|B4 A2|F4 A2|",
  "genre": "ITRAD",
  "deleted": false
}
```

Swagger UI and curl can also successfully send that patch.

If I remove the accented characters and turn them into "i"s and "o", the axios version works perfectly.  If I just send "í" the request will fail, then if I change that to "i" it will succeed.

Using wireshark I can see the data is bogus `[Path with value: /title:S� Bheag, S� Mh�r]` where with the `fetch` version on wireshark it looks like `[Path with value: /title:Sí Bheag, Sí Mhór]`. 

I've googled, searched the issues (I do see https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/5757), queried CoPilot using multiple models and Gemini too.    I've tried using `transformRequest` in many variations to override the encoding.  

Additonal info: localhost:8000, vanilla http, FastAPI@0.115.6 serving on the backend (requests never get to the middleware), Node v22.7.0, axios@1.7.9, no intercepters or default settings, MacBook Pro M3 Max Sequoia 15.2.


### To Reproduce

The information in the description should be enough to reproduce.  At least tonight, I don't quite have the heart and energy to create a fully code-complete program.

### Code snippet

```js
const response = await axios.patch<ITune>(urlString, tuneUpdateData, {
      headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      },
      timeout: 10_000,
    });
});
```

### Expected behavior

It should not throw and error, and the server should receive the PATCH request.

1.7.9

xhr2 I guess, whatever the default is
